message.content:
You are an OPUS BUILDER executing ONE phase of Sol's authoritative build plan for POLARIS's fabrication-safety validator.

PLAN FILE (read it first): /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md
YOUR PHASE: P2 — "Unify version derivation and quarantine impossible pairs"
ALREADY-LANDED PHASES (do not redo): P1, P1S
REPO: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel

DO EXACTLY THIS:
1. Read /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/sota_review/foundation/SOL_BUILD_PLAN.md. Find the section for phase P2, and also read "## Global rules for every phase".
2. Implement the phase's EXACT edits in the files it names. Follow Sol's spec PRECISELY — do NOT invent mechanisms or substitute your own design. If the plan says "one reducer", unify to one; if it says "declarative table", use a table; there is NO rank scheme. 
3. Honor the GLOBAL RULES: generality (NO task-72 / no DOI, title, author, journal, or subject literals in any rule — rules fire by structural/typed signal only), positive-proof only (a gate may reject on absence but ADMIT only on positive evidence), and add the phase's required metamorphic/generality test.
4. Run the phase's ACCEPTANCE TEST exactly as the plan specifies. It MUST exit 0.
5. If it fails, FIX your implementation until the acceptance test passes — do NOT weaken or delete the test to make it pass. If you truly cannot make it pass, stop and report the blocker with the failing output.
6. Do NOT git commit. Do NOT work on other phases. Do NOT disturb outputs/ blobs, the ledger, or launch any long-running/LLM process.

SAFETY (paramount): this code decides whether 3.5M words of new corpus can be cited without fabrication. NEVER make a change that could let a DIFFERENT_WORK or UNRESOLVED_BINDING manifestation be attributed to a claimed source, or let a preprint/working-paper be cited as the journal. When uncertain, quarantine / fail closed. A prior session already hand-made partial "foundation" edits (identity gate in resolve_attribution, from_json re-derivation, evidence_miner ~line 1401 fixed to resolve_attribution(binding,policy), factored derive_binding_core, IDENTITY_PROVEN allowlist) — P1 hardens/verifies these; later phases build on them.

Return the schema: implemented (bool), acceptance_passed (did the test exit 0), the exact test_command, test_output_tail (~last 20 lines), files_changed, a one-paragraph summary, and blocker (empty string if none).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the build plan, focusing on P2 and the global rules.

content.content:
1	# Binding-gate build plan
2	
3	I reviewed the authoritative design note and the live tree. The six hand edits are directionally correct, but they are not yet sufficient to ship. The remaining critical issues are:
4	
5	- Two independent version derivations still disagree.
6	- Generic call paths still default to `JOURNAL_ONLY`.
7	- Prompt-derived source policy can be lost in fallback contract paths.
8	- Correspondence still trusts the old whole-body `identity.verdict`.
9	- Unresolved/different works still reach the expensive mining lane.
10	- Machine-readable self-metadata is not yet used for positive salvage.
11	- The composer and argument planner still contain live task-specific defaults.
12	
13	The workflow should execute the eight gated phases below in order: P1, P1S, P2–P7. P1S is the explicit source-policy phase required by the operator; numbering P2–P7 remains aligned with the requested phase names.
14	
15	## Global rules for every phase
16	
17	Every phase must preserve these invariants:
18	
19	1. No DOI, title, author, venue, journal, topic, benchmark ID, or subject string may participate in a production decision.
20	2. Decisions may depend only on typed work metadata, artifact structure, self-identifying metadata, byte-derived observations, verified span relationships, and the original prompt.
21	3. The identity gate is always active and is independent of source policy.
22	4. Missing, malformed, stale, or unknown values fail closed.
23	5. `JOURNAL_ONLY` is selected only from a positive constraint in the original prompt. Otherwise use `ANY_VERSION`.
24	6. Each phase must add at least one metamorphic test where identifiers and subject vocabulary are replaced while structure is held constant.
25	7. Do not tune production rules to make the 501-row cohort count come out correctly. Cohort counts are regression observations, not rule inputs.
26	8. Do not advance to the next phase until the phase command exits zero.
27	
28	---
29	
30	## P1 — Finish and harden the six foundation edits
31	
32	### Files
33	
34	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
35	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
36	- [scripts/evidence_miner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py)
37	- [scripts/cellcog_composer.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/cellcog_composer.py)
38	- [scripts/argument_planner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/argument_planner.py)
39	- [scripts/synthesis_contract.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/synthesis_contract.py)
40	- New: `tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py`
41	- New: `tests/test_runtime_contract_generality.py`
42	
43	### Exact edits
44	
45	#### 1. `derive_binding_core` and `IDENTITY_PROVEN`
46	
47	Keep both additions in `event_ledger.py`.
48	
49	Correct the string comparison:
50	
51	```python
52	if binding is SAME_WORK:
53	```
54	
55	must become:
56	
57	```python
58	if binding == SAME_WORK:
59	```
60	
61	Keep `derive_semantic_binding(events)` as a thin wrapper. No other identity reducer may be introduced.
62	
63	All verdict consumers must import or call the same `IDENTITY_PROVEN`; do not reproduce its members in another tuple.
64	
65	#### 2. Identity derivation in `ingest_bytes()`
66	
67	Keep the live `observe_text(text) → derive_binding_core(...)` path and the stored:
68	
69	```python
70	profile["semantic_binding"]
71	profile["semantic_binding_basis"]
72	profile["semantic_binding_derived_by"]
73	```
74	
75	Keep `DIFFERENT_WORK` on the quarantine-expression path. It must:
76	
77	- create no claimed-expression edge;
78	- use `kind="unknown"`;
79	- use an expression ID structurally derived from work ID and disposition;
80	- retain the manifestation bytes.
81	
82	Do not copy `row["semantic_binding"]` into each manifestation.
83	
84	In `migrate()`, compare a row-level cached verdict only to the row’s selected `fulltext_manifestation`, when that relationship is explicitly present. Store the comparison as audit-only data:
85	
86	```python
87	profile["row_binding_cache"] = {
88	    "stored": ...,
89	    "live": ...,
90	    "agrees": bool,
91	}
92	```
93	
94	No resolver or constructor may read this field to grant permission.
95	
96	#### 3. `Attribution` fields
97	
98	Keep:
99	
100	```python
101	identity_verdict
102	disposition
103	reason_code
104	```
105	
106	Define stable constants for dispositions and reason codes in `provenance.py`; do not scatter string literals through callers.
107	
108	Stamp every resolver return, including invalid bindings:
109	
110	- invalid span: `QUARANTINE / SPAN_BINDING_INVALID`;
111	- different work: `QUARANTINE / IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK`;
112	- unresolved: `LEAD_ONLY / IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED`;
113	- missing/unknown verdict: `QUARANTINE / IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT`;
114	- incomplete: `QUARANTINE / INCOMPLETE_BYTES`;
115	- policy mismatch: `LEAD_ONLY / VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED`;
116	- successful resolution: `ADMIT / ADMITTED`.
117	
118	Every refusal must have:
119	
120	```python
121	admitted is False
122	names_expression_id is None
123	text is None
124	permitted_expression_ids == ()
125	```
126	
127	for identity/integrity failures.
128	
129	#### 4. Resolver identity gate
130	
131	Keep its location after span verification and before completeness and expression policy.
132	
133	Remove the unused local imports of each version constant. Import only the canonical allowlist and the two explicit negative verdicts.
134	
135	Do not compute a permissive fallback from the legacy `profile["identity"]["verdict"]`.
136	
137	#### 5. Strict JSON load
138	
139	Keep live re-derivation and refusal on disagreement, but tighten it:
140	
141	- Missing stored `semantic_binding` is an integrity error, not an old-file compatibility path.
142	- Unknown stored verdict is an integrity error.
143	- Re-derive both verdict and basis.
144	- Use the live verdict and live basis after validation.
145	- A stored row-cache verdict is never used.
146	- Update `_take()` to recognize dataclass defaults mechanically with `dataclasses.MISSING`; do not maintain a special handwritten list such as `{"profile", "kind"}`.
147	
148	Migration is the compatibility path for legacy corpus rows. Strict graph load must not silently migrate an old graph.
149	
150	#### 6. Miner binding call
151	
152	Keep the corrected call:
153	
154	```python
155	graph.resolve_attribution(binding, policy)
156	```
157	
158	Add a regression proving that exact-span correspondence is visible only when the binding, rather than the bare manifestation ID, is passed.
159	
160	#### Remove live task-specific runtime defaults
161	
162	The current downstream runtime still contains task-specific production values.
163	
164	In `cellcog_composer.py`:
165	
166	- Delete the fixed AI/labor `WRITE_PROMPT` subject.
167	- Delete the fixed `OUTLINE`.
168	- Delete the fixed report title.
169	- Change writer input to accept the compiled `research_contract.Contract`, its question/title, and `research_contract.derive_outline(...)`.
170	- Render source-method prose from `Contract.source_policy.compliance_prose()`.
171	- Card instructions must say “permitted source” or the derived policy description, not always “peer-reviewed journal article.”
172	- `abstract_nodes()` and `methods_nodes()` must receive the contract and derive their wording from it.
173	
174	In `argument_planner.py`:
175	
176	- Remove the production `default_contract()` containing AI/labor facets.
177	- Replace it with `contract_from_research_contract(contract, outline)`.
178	- A no-contract call produces an empty, facet-agnostic contract, not an AI contract.
179	- Any AI fixture belongs under tests or an explicitly named demo helper used only by `__main__`.
180	
181	In `synthesis_contract.py`:
182	
183	- The historical task-specific members have already been removed from `SAFE_CAPS`; preserve that.
184	- Delete the `SAFE_CAPS` authority entirely from production validation.
185	- Capitalized names are permitted only when present in a premise, in the original question/compiled contract, sentence-initial by orthography, or emitted from a typed renderer enum.
186	- Replace fixed `LEVEL_CUES` as an authority with per-contract facet matchers or span receipts. Fixed social-science vocabulary must not decide clinical, legal, or CS evidence.
187	- `argument_planner.owned_is_safe()` must use the same structural predicate, not import a second allowlist.
188	
189	Historical examples may remain in comments and tests, but no runtime default may contain or select the task subject.
190	
191	### Acceptance test
192	
193	Create foundation tests covering:
194	
195	- live foreign DOI derives `DIFFERENT_WORK`;
196	- changing only requested DOI makes the same bytes reject under both policies;
197	- changing row cache from `DIFFERENT_WORK` to `SAME_WORK` does not change live resolution;
198	- missing and unknown verdicts fail closed;
199	- invalid binding is structurally stamped;
200	- JSON verdict tampering is refused;
201	- the corrected `gate_card()` passes its binding.
202	
203	Generality test:
204	
205	- compile and render one clinical, one legal, and one CS prompt;
206	- none may inherit AI, labor-market, journal-only, or 4IR wording;
207	- a capitalized entity absent from premises/contract is rejected;
208	- replacing it with a completely different entity produces the same structural result;
209	- putting that entity into a premise makes it admissible without adding it to an allowlist.
210	
211	Gate:
212	
213	```bash
214	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
215	PYTHONPATH=scripts python -m pytest -q \
216	  tests/test_binding_gate_foundation.py \
217	  tests/test_runtime_contract_generality.py
218	PYTHONPATH=scripts python scripts/provenance.py
219	```
220	
221	### Depends on
222	
223	None.
224	
225	---
226	
227	## P1S — Infer the adjustable source policy from the original prompt
228	
229	### Files
230	
231	- [scripts/research_contract.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/research_contract.py)
232	- [scripts/evidence_miner.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/evidence_miner.py)
233	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
234	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
235	- New: `tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py`
236	
237	### Exact prompt-derivation rule
238	
239	Add a deterministic, clause-scoped function in `research_contract.py`:
240	
241	```python
242	derive_version_scope(question: str) -> tuple[str, list[str]]
243	```
244	
245	It returns `("JOURNAL_ONLY", evidence_clauses)` only when a clause positively directs the system to use published, peer-reviewed, or journal sources.
246	
247	A positive directive requires both:
248	
249	1. A source-class phrase:
250	
251	```text
252	journal article/source/publication/literature
253	peer-reviewed article/source/publication/literature
254	published article/study/source/paper
255	```
256	
257	2. A directive or exclusivity relation in the same clause:
258	
259	```text
260	use, cite, include, draw on, rely on, restrict to, limit to,
261	must be, should be, ensure sources are, only, exclusively, solely
262	```
263	
264	Negation or widening in the same clause defeats `JOURNAL_ONLY`, including:
265	
266	```text
267	not limited to journals
268	do not restrict to journals
269	include preprints/working papers/accepted manuscripts
270	journal articles and preprints
271	```
272	
273	Mere discussion of journals, publication, peer review, or comparisons between source types does not set the gate.
274	
275	No source-class demand means `ANY_VERSION`.
276	
277	The function must return the exact verbatim clause(s) that justified the restriction. The LLM contract compiler may not invent, remove, or broaden this value.
278	
279	### Wiring
280	
281	In `research_contract.SourcePolicy`, add:
282	
283	```python
284	version_scope: str = "ANY_VERSION"
285	version_scope_evidence: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
286	```
287	
288	`_floor_source_policy(question)` sets these exclusively through `derive_version_scope(question)`.
289	
290	In `evidence_miner.load_contract()`:
291	
292	- Every return path—explicit facets, compiled contract, cache, question-term fallback, and no-question fallback—must pass through one finalizer.
293	- The finalizer derives provenance policy from the original `question`, not from the most recently cached contract.
294	- `JOURNAL_ONLY` maps to `provenance.JOURNAL_ONLY`.
295	- `ANY_VERSION` maps to `provenance.ANY_VERSION`.
296	- Remove the current inference from `peer_reviewed_only + excluded_types`.
297	- Remove the `PEER_REVIEWED` intermediate behavior from this two-state version policy.
298	
299	Correct generic defaults:
300	
301	```python
302	Graph.resolve_attribution(..., source_policy=ANY_VERSION)
303	gate_card(...): ... or P.ANY_VERSION
304	MiningContract.source_policy: ANY_VERSION
305	```
306	
307	Keep `journal_attributable()` explicit about passing `JOURNAL_ONLY`.
308	
309	In `mine()`:
310	
311	- The contract-derived policy is authoritative when `question` is nonempty.
312	- An explicit `source_policy` that disagrees with a nonempty prompt-derived policy raises `ValueError`.
313	- Explicit policy remains allowed for low-level tests with `question == ""`.
314	
315	In `event_ledger.py`:
316	
317	- Replace `journal_articles_only: bool = True` in `derive_eligibility()` and `record_eligibility()`.
318	- Accept an explicit `SourcePolicy`, defaulting to `ANY_VERSION`.
319	- Version eligibility must consult the policy’s permitted expression kinds.
320	- A preprint is not unconditionally a discovery lead under `ANY_VERSION`.
321	- Remove task-number language from function semantics.
322	
323	### Acceptance test
324	
325	The same valid, complete preprint fixture must produce:
326	
327	| Prompt | Policy | Result |
328	|---|---|---|
329	| “Summarize the evidence about X.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
330	| “Use journal articles and preprints about X.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
331	| “Explain differences between journals and preprint servers.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT`, names preprint |
332	| “Use only peer-reviewed sources about X.” | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `LEAD_ONLY` |
333	| “Cite published journal articles about X.” | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `LEAD_ONLY` |
334	| “Do not limit this to journal articles; include working papers.” | `ANY_VERSION` | `ADMIT` |
335	
336	For `JOURNAL_ONLY`, assert:
337	
338	```python
339	admitted is False
340	disposition == "LEAD_ONLY"
341	reason_code == "VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED"
342	names_expression_id is None
343	```
344	
345	Metamorphic test: replace X with unrelated clinical, legal, economics, and CS subjects; policy must not change.
346	
347	Gate:
348	
349	```bash
350	cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel
351	PYTHONPATH=scripts python -m pytest -q tests/test_source_policy_derivation.py
352	```
353	
354	### Depends on
355	
356	P1.
357	
358	---
359	
360	## P2 — Unify version derivation and quarantine impossible pairs
361	
362	### Design decision
363	
364	Use one version reducer. Do not retain two competing derivations and choose between them later.
365	
366	`version_evidence()` becomes the single reducer that returns both:
367	
368	```python
369	VersionDecision(
370	    semantic_binding,
371	    expression_kind,
372	    evidence_key,
373	    basis,
374	)
375	```
376	
377	`ingest_bytes()` must assign its expression kind from this result. `derive_binding_core()` must use the same result when identity is proven.
378	
379	The resolver still validates the resulting pair, but that is an invariant check against tampering or in-memory corruption—not a second version derivation.
380	
381	### Files
382	
383	- [scripts/event_ledger.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/event_ledger.py)
384	- [scripts/provenance.py](/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/provenance.py)
385	- New: `tests/test_version_derivation_unification.py`
386	
387	### Exact edits
388	
389	In `event_ledger.py`:
390	
391	- Replace `VERSION_EVIDENCE` triples with typed ordered rules that specify both semantic binding and expression kind.
392	- Split working-paper and preprint observations structurally.
393	- Preserve precedence:
394	
395	```text
396	accepted manuscript
397	working paper
398	preprint
399	published/proceedings version
400	no version evidence
401	```
402	
403	- Cover-sheet and article-front voices remain separately recorded.
404	- Accepted/preprint/working evidence vetoes published evidence.
405	- Repository names may be registry data, but no DOI, title, author, journal, or subject may appear in the reducer.
406	- For non-scholarly typed works, derive `official_text` or `registry_record` from `Work.kind`, not scholarly furniture.
407	
408	In `provenance.py`:
409	
410	- `profile()` may retain artifact diagnostics, but it must not independently decide the expression kind.
411	- Make `derive_expression_kind()` a compatibility wrapper over the shared observations and `version_evidence()`, or remove it after all callers migrate.
412	- In `ingest_bytes()`, obtain `VersionDecision` once and use its `expression_kind` and basis.
413	- Store the decision’s evidence key in the profile.
414	- In `Graph.from_json()`, re-run the same reducer and refuse disagreement with:
415	  - stored expression kind;
416	  - the manifestation’s expression node kind;
417	  - stored semantic version when identity is proven.
418	- In `resolve_attribution()`, after identity and before completeness, validate the pair against one declarative compatibility table.
419	- Any incompatible pair returns:
420	
421	```python
422	admitted=False
423	disposition="QUARANTINE"
424	reason_code="DERIVATION_CONFLICT"
425	names_expression_id=None
426	text=None
427	permitted_expression_ids=()
428	```
429	
430	### Exact pair decision table
431	
432	Assume valid span, identity-proven bytes, and completeness. Correspondence exceptions apply only to the exact bound span.
433	
434	| Semantic binding | Own expression kind | Pair status | `JOURNAL_ONLY` | `ANY_VERSION` |
435	|---|---|---|---|---|
436	| `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED` | `journal_version` | consistent | ADMIT journal | ADMIT journal |
437	| `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED` | `proceedings_version` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT proceedings |
438	| `VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED` | `accepted_manuscript` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT accepted manuscript |
439	| `VERSION_OF_PREPRINT` | `working_paper` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT working paper |
440	| `VERSION_OF_PREPRINT` | `preprint` | consistent | LEAD_ONLY; exact verified journal correspondence may admit that span | ADMIT preprint |
441	| `SAME_WORK` | `official_text` for typed case/statute | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT official text |
442	| `SAME_WORK` | `registry_record` for typed trial/registry work | consistent | LEAD_ONLY | ADMIT registry record |
443	| `SAME_WORK` | `unknown` | unresolved version, not conflict | LEAD_ONLY | LEAD_ONLY |
444	| Any proven semantic version | Any other own expression kind | conflict | QUARANTINE | QUARANTINE |
445	
446	In particular:
447	
448	```text
449	VERSION_OF_PREPRINT + journal_version = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
450	VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED + journal_version = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
451	VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED + working_paper/preprint/accepted_manuscript = DERIVATION_CONFLICT
452	```
453	
454	There is no rank scheme and no “choose the safer-looking label.”
455	
456	### Metamorphic acceptance vector
457	
458	Create one accepted fixture, then mutate only structural version furniture:
459	
460	1. Published furniture → `VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED / journal_version`.
461	2. Add accepted-manuscript stamp → both outputs change to accepted.
462	3. Replace accepted stamp with working-paper stamp → both outputs change to preprint/working.
463	4. Relabel the graph expression node to `journal_version` without changing bytes → loader refuses and resolver returns `DERIVATION_CONFLICT`.
464	5. Repeat with unrelated clinical, legal, and CS titles/authors; results remain identical.
465	
466	The real seven known conflicts must become zero admissible conflicts without listing their identifiers.
467

631	### Exact data model
632	
633	Add an explicit `IdentityReceipt` dataclass containing at least:
634	
635	```python

655	For field-based PDF metadata, offsets are offsets inside the named metadata field. For XMP/HTML/JATS, offsets are byte or decoded-character offsets in the raw artifact. The location type must make the coordinate space explicit.
656	
657	Extend `Manifestation` with optional raw-artifact lineage:

667	
668	`migrate()` matches the selected `fulltext_manifestation` or exact `text_sha256` against `row["manifestations"]` and passes its raw blob reference. It must not copy a receipt or verdict from the row.
669	
670	### Exact extraction rule
671	
672	Run `reresolve_unresolved_metadata(graph, blob_store)` only after baseline manifestations exist, and only over live `UNRESOLVED_BINDING`.
673	
674	Admissible self-fields are:
675	
676	#### PDF, through PyMuPDF/fitz
677	
678	- DOI: PDF Info or XMP self-metadata fields.
679	- Title: PDF Info/XMP title.
680	- Authors: PDF Info/XMP author/creator.
681	
682	Do not inspect rendered body or reference text.
683	
684	#### HTML
685	
686	Inside `<head>` only:
687	
688	- identifier: `citation_doi`, `DC.identifier`;
689	- title: `citation_title`, `DC.title`;
690	- author: repeated `citation_author`, `DC.creator`.
691	
692	#### JATS
693	
694	Inside `<front><article-meta>` only:
695	
696	- identifier: `<article-id pub-id-type="doi">`;
697	- title: `<article-title>`;
698	- authors: `<contrib contrib-type="author">`, using structured name fields.
699	
700	Never search `<body>` or `<back>`.
701	
702	### Positive promotion rule
703	
704	Normalize DOI by parsing DOI URI/prefix forms, case-folding, percent-decoding, and stripping terminal citation punctuation.
705	
706	Normalize title with Unicode NFKC, entity decoding, case-folding, punctuation-to-space, and whitespace collapse. Title match is exact after normalization—no fuzzy threshold.
707	
708	Normalize authors structurally:
709	
710	- compare requested family name to a structured surname field; or
711	- compare an exact normalized full-name sequence;
712	- a single requested surname must equal a metadata surname token, not be a substring.
713	
714	Promote only when one of these holds:
715	
716	1. Exact requested DOI in a permitted self-identifier field.
717	2. Exact normalized requested title and at least one requested author in permitted self-identity fields.
718	
719	Conflict rule:
720	
721	- if self-identifier fields contain both target and foreign DOI values, remain unresolved with `IDENTITY_METADATA_CONFLICT`;
722	- title/author evidence cannot override a conflicting self-identifier;
723	- absence of metadata changes nothing.
724	
725	A verified receipt becomes an observation consumed by `derive_binding_core()`. The reducer then re-derives `SAME_WORK` or the appropriate version verdict. Do not directly assign a promoted verdict in the salvage loop.
726	
727	### Loader revalidation
728	
729	`Graph.from_json(..., blob_store=...)` must:
730	
731	1. load raw bytes by immutable blob ID;
732	2. verify raw hash;
733	3. rerun the named metadata extractor/version;
734	4. re-find the exact raw match at the recorded location;
735	5. re-normalize the value;
736	6. re-evaluate it against the Work;
737	7. compare the canonical live receipt to the stored receipt;
738	8. re-run `derive_binding_core()` using verified receipts;
739	9. refuse the graph on any mismatch or unavailable receipt artifact.
740	
741	A graph with receipts cannot load without access to the referenced blob store.
742	
743	### OCR status
744	
745	Do not implement OCR.
746	
747	Document in `docs/identity_metadata_salvage.md`:

751	reason: no installed/revalidatable OCR backend; tesseract unavailable and no sudo
752	effect: residual glyph-header manifestations remain LEAD_ONLY
753	```
754	
755	An `OCR`-typed receipt supplied through JSON must be refused as an unsupported receipt type.
