1098	- approximately 67 unresolved manifestations have machine-metadata receipts and approximately 88 remain leads;
1099	- no OCR promotion is claimed;
1100	- the composer’s title, outline, methods prose, and source wording are derived from the current contract rather than task-specific runtime constants.

content.text: Now let me read the key source files. Let me start with event_ledger.py and provenance.py.

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""EVENT LEDGER — every status is a PURE REDUCER over an append-only event log.
3	
4	    Sol, item 8: "NO COMPONENT MAY WRITE `complete`, `fulltext`, `no evidence`, `same work`,
5	                  or `high quality` DIRECTLY."
6	
7	THE FAILURE THIS IS THE STRUCTURAL CURE FOR
8	
9	  Every defect we have shipped had ONE shape: A LABEL THAT ASSERTED MORE THAN ITS CONTENT
10	  SUPPORTED, WITH NOTHING CHECKING. Not one announced itself; each read as a fact about the world.
11	
12	      "gate: WIRED"          -> it checked the wrong lane; fabrication shipped
13	      "span-verified"        -> verified by its FIRST 60 CHARACTERS
14	      "still paywalled"      -> we asked by DOI; the free copy is a SEPARATE WORK
15	      "FULLTEXT"             -> 535 words. A cookie banner.
16	      "no free copy exists"  -> we were HTTP 429/404. A fact about OUR REQUEST and THEIR INDEX,
17	                                disguised as a fact about the world.
18	      "working_paper"        -> written by whichever SCRIPT ran, not by what the document IS.
19	
20	  A component that CAN assert its own success will eventually assert it falsely, and nothing will
21	  catch it. So here a component may ONLY EMIT EVENTS — observations, in the vocabulary of the world:
22	  an HTTP status, a word count, a byline, a glyph ratio. The LABEL is DERIVED from those events by a
23	  reducer nobody can bypass, and the label is never stored anywhere a component could overwrite it.
24	
25	THE TWO RULES THAT MAKE IT STRUCTURAL, NOT ADVISORY
26	
27	  1. EMIT-TIME CONCLUSION GUARD. `Ledger.emit()` REJECTS any event whose payload carries a terminal
28	     conclusion — a key like `content_status`, or a value like "FULLTEXT" / "same work" /
29	     "no evidence" / "high quality". You cannot write the label into the ledger even if you try.
30	     Raw text quoted from the world (`header_text`, `span`, `document_title`) is exempt: a fetched
31	     PDF is allowed to contain the word "complete". A COMPONENT is not.
32	
33	  2. LABELS ARE NEVER STORED. They are computed on read by `derive_*` pure functions over the event
34	     list. There is no field to corrupt, no cache to go stale, and no way to "just set it".
35	
36	THE DISTINCTIONS THAT MUST NEVER COLLAPSE (Sol)
37	
38	    HTTP 429/503        -> BACKEND_FAILED.  ** NOT "no evidence exists". **
39	    HTTP 404            -> NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND. A fact about THEIR INDEX. Also not absence.
40	    route_complete      -> EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
41	                           It must NEVER mean "an adapter was mapped."
42	    a budget stop       -> IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP.
43	    absence             -> only an ADEQUATE route (every adapter genuinely answered, none throttled,
44	                           none blocked, no budget stop) can support a scoped absence statement.
45	    "high_quality"      -> FORBIDDEN as a bare label. It renders as its COMPONENTS, each with
46	                           provenance: directness=high, method_quality=low, influence_percentile=0.92.
47	
48	Run it:
49	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py            # self-test + replay of our real history
50	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py --replay   # just the replay
51	    python3 scripts/event_ledger.py --selftest # just the invariants
52	"""
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import json
56	import re
57	import sys
58	import time
59	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
60	from pathlib import Path
61	from typing import Any, Iterable
62	
63	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
64	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))
65	
66	# THE REGISTRY, AND THE ONE COMPLETENESS REDUCER. Imported — never re-implemented. This module used to
67	# carry its own `FULLTEXT_MIN = 2500` while provenance.py used 1,200, and neither knew about the
68	# other. See the headstone at "3. content profile", below.
69	from provenance import (  # noqa: E402
70	    KIND_PROFILE, judge_completeness,
71	    # THE TWO-STATE SOURCE POLICY, shared with the resolver: eligibility consults the policy's own
72	    # `permitted_expression_kinds`, so the ledger and the gate can never disagree about what a version
73	    # may be cited as. ANY_VERSION is the default — a preprint is a discovery lead ONLY when the policy
74	    # in force does not permit its kind, never unconditionally.
75	    SourcePolicy as _SourcePolicy, ANY_VERSION as _ANY_VERSION,
76	    SOURCE_TYPE as _SOURCE_TYPE, WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT as _WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT,
77	    # ONE definition of "what is a repository cover sheet", shared with the provenance lane. Two
78	    # copies of this pattern would drift, and the one that drifts is always the one a gate reads.
79	    _COVER_SHEET,
80	    # ...and ONE definition of "which stamps mark an accepted manuscript", for the SAME reason. This
81	    # used to be a SECOND copy (`_ACCEPTED_STAMP`, hand-maintained here) and the two DID drift: hop 6
82	    # widened provenance._AM_MARK to catch the NIH author-manuscript tells ("author manuscript;
83	    # available in PMC", "NIHMS<digits>") and this file's copy never learned them, so an NIH accepted
84	    # manuscript that provenance ruled `accepted_manuscript` was scored VERSION_PUBLISHED here and
85	    # became journal evidence. There is now ONE detector, imported, so the two lanes CANNOT diverge.
86	    _AM_MARK as _ACCEPTED_STAMP,
87	)
88	
89	CORPUS = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'journal_corpus_content.json'
90	LEDGER_OUT = ROOT / 'outputs' / 'event_ledger.jsonl'
91	
92	
93	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
94	# EVENTS — the only thing a component may write.
95	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
96	
97	class EventKind:
98	    """Sol's twelve, verbatim, plus the budget stop (which must be distinguishable from a gap)."""
99	    ROUTE_PLANNED             = 'route_planned'
100	    BACKEND_ATTEMPTED         = 'backend_attempted'
101	    RESPONSE_RECEIVED         = 'response_received'
102	    THROTTLED                 = 'throttled'
103	    BLOCKED                   = 'blocked'
104	    CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED      = 'candidate_identified'
105	    MANIFESTATION_FETCHED     = 'manifestation_fetched'
106	    CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED   = 'content_profile_derived'
107	    SEMANTIC_BINDING_DECIDED  = 'semantic_binding_decided'
108	    ELIGIBILITY_DECIDED       = 'eligibility_decided'
109	    WEIGHT_COMPONENTS_DERIVED = 'weight_components_derived'
110	    COVERAGE_STATUS_DERIVED   = 'coverage_status_derived'
111	    # "a budget stop IS NOT AN EVIDENCE GAP" — so it needs its own event, or it cannot be told apart.
112	    BUDGET_STOPPED            = 'budget_stopped'
113	
114	    ALL = {ROUTE_PLANNED, BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, RESPONSE_RECEIVED, THROTTLED, BLOCKED,
115	           CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED, MANIFESTATION_FETCHED, CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED,
116	           SEMANTIC_BINDING_DECIDED, ELIGIBILITY_DECIDED, WEIGHT_COMPONENTS_DERIVED,
117	           COVERAGE_STATUS_DERIVED, BUDGET_STOPPED}
118	
119	
120	class ForbiddenLabel(Exception):
121	    """A component tried to write a conclusion into the ledger. That is the whole disease."""
122	
123	
124	#: Terminal conclusions. A component may not state these — the reducer derives them.
125	#:
126	#: Sol V9 §1, verbatim: "The adapter must not write FULLTEXT, THIS_WORK, VERSION_OF_RECORD, ADMISSIBLE,
127	#: or an expression edge. Those are reducer outputs derived from immutable metadata and document bytes."
128	#: FULLTEXT, ADMISSIBLE and `same work` were already here. THIS_WORK and VERSION_OF_RECORD were not —
129	#: and an adapter that may write `version_of_record` into a payload has been handed, by the back door,
130	#: precisely the authority the V9 P0 was about taking away from it. A repository saying `acceptedVersion`
131	#: is an OBSERVATION; a component writing `version_of_record` is a VERDICT.
132	RESERVED_VALUES = (
133	    'fulltext', 'full text', 'full-text',
134	    'complete', 'route_complete',
135	    'no evidence', 'no free copy', 'no free text', 'not available', 'nothing found',
136	    'same work', 'same_work', 'this work', 'this_work', 'different work', 'different_work',
137	    'version equivalent', 'version of record', 'version_of_record',
138	    'high quality', 'high_quality', 'low quality', 'low_quality',
139	    'paywalled', 'still paywalled',
140	    'supported', 'searched_none', 'conflicted',
141	    'admissible', 'inadmissible', 'eligible',
142	    'verified', 'span-verified', 'fabrication-proof',
143	)
144	
145	#: Keys that ARE labels. Naming one is an attempt to set the label directly.
146	RESERVED_KEYS = (
147	    'content_status', 'status', 'label', 'verdict', 'quality', 'tier', 'grade',
148	    'fulltext_source', 'is_fulltext', 'route_complete', 'complete', 'coverage',
149	    'coverage_status', 'eligibility', 'binding', 'admissible', 'evidence_status',
150	)
151	
152	#: Fields that are RAW TEXT QUOTED FROM THE WORLD. A PDF may contain any word at all; that is not
153	#: the component asserting it. These are exempt from the value scan (never from the key scan).
154	#: NOTE what is NOT here: `note`. A free-text `note` is the COMPONENT talking, not the world, and it
155	#: is precisely where a conclusion would be smuggled in ("note: no free copy exists"). It stays
156	#: scanned. (The guard caught exactly that in this file's own replay code, and the fix was to reword
157	#: the note into an observation — not to exempt the field.)
158	#: `locator` is here for the same reason `url` is: IT IS A URL. Publishers put our own reserved words
159	#: inside their paths (`/articles/PMC.../?report=fulltext`, `/science/article/.../fulltext`), and a
160	#: guard that refused to record THE ADDRESS THE BYTES CAME FROM — because the address contains the
161	#: string "fulltext" — would have made the locator unrecordable and left us exactly where we were:
162	#: holding an NBER working paper under a URL pointing at aeaweb.org.
163	#: `requested_venue` sits beside `requested_title` for the same reason: it is a NAME, quoted from the
164	#: bibliography. There is a journal called `Information & Management` and there could be one called
165	#: `Complete Systems`; a guard that refused to record which journal we asked about, because the
166	#: journal's name contains one of our reserved words, would be a guard against nothing.
167	#: `article_front_matter` and `cover_sheet_text` are the two halves segment_front_matter() carves out
168	#: of the fetched document (observe_text, below) — RAW DOCUMENT TEXT, in the same category as
169	#: `header_text` and `identity_window`. A real article's front matter routinely contains a reserved
170	#: word ("Complete list of authors" on a PubMed page, a paper whose title includes "validated" or
171	#: "supported"), and a guard that refused to record the document's own front matter because the
172	#: document contains one of our words would silently DROP the paper — the exact failure this project
173	#: exists to end. They are exempt from the VALUE scan (never from the key scan), like every other
174	#: quote from the world.
175	VERBATIM_KEYS = frozenset({
176	    'header_text', 'identity_window', 'span', 'document_title', 'byline', 'snippet', 'raw', 'title',
177	    'reason_text', 'requested_title', 'requested_venue', 'url', 'locator', 'doi', 'venue',
178	    'article_front_matter', 'cover_sheet_text',
179	})
180	
181	
182	def _scan_value(k: str, v, bad: list[str], path: str = '') -> None:
183	    """Scan ONE value, RECURSIVELY.
184	
185	    IT DID NOT RECURSE. The scan ran over `payload.items()` and tested `isinstance(v, str)` — so a
186	    conclusion one level down was invisible to it:
187	
188	        emit(..., adapter_observations={'content_status': 'FULLTEXT'})    # ACCEPTED. Silently.
189	
190	    A guard that only inspects the top level of a nested structure is a guard you step over by adding
191	    a dict, and "the checks certify a lane the fabrication no longer uses" is the sentence this whole
192	    project is trying to stop writing. Payloads stay flat by convention; this makes it true by force.
193	    """
194	    where = f'{path}{k}'
195	    if isinstance(v, str):
196	        vl = v.lower().strip()
197	        for r in RESERVED_VALUES:
198	            # match the whole value or a word-bounded occurrence — not a substring of a word
199	            if vl == r or re.search(rf'(?<![a-z0-9_]){re.escape(r)}(?![a-z0-9_])', vl):
200	                bad.append(f'{where}={v!r} states the conclusion {r!r}; emit the OBSERVATION instead '
201	                           f'(word_count / http_status / byline), and let the reducer conclude')
202	                return
203	    elif isinstance(v, dict):
204	        for k2, v2 in v.items():
205	            if str(k2).lower() in RESERVED_KEYS:
206	                bad.append(f'key {where}.{k2!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may '
207	                           f'not set it, and burying it one level down does not make it an observation')
208	                continue
209	            if str(k2).lower() in VERBATIM_KEYS:
210	                continue
211	            _scan_value(str(k2), v2, bad, path=f'{where}.')
212	    elif isinstance(v, (list, tuple)):
213	        for i, v2 in enumerate(v):
214	            _scan_value(f'[{i}]', v2, bad, path=where)
215	
216	
217	@dataclass(frozen=True)
218	class Event:
219	    """An OBSERVATION. Immutable, ordered, and never a conclusion."""
220	    seq: int
221	    unit: str                    # what this is about — a DOI, a coverage cell id, a route id
222	    kind: str
223	    actor: str                   # which component observed it (for provenance, not for authority)
224	    payload: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
225	    ts: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
226	
227	    def to_json(self) -> str:
228	        return json.dumps({'seq': self.seq, 'unit': self.unit, 'kind': self.kind,
229	                           'actor': self.actor, 'payload': self.payload, 'ts': self.ts},
230	                          sort_keys=True)
231	
232	
233	def _scan_for_conclusions(kind: str, payload: dict) -> list[str]:
234	    """Return the reasons this payload is an ASSERTION rather than an OBSERVATION."""
235	    bad: list[str] = []
236	    for k, v in payload.items():
237	        kl = str(k).lower()
238	        if kl in RESERVED_KEYS:
239	            bad.append(f'key {k!r} IS a label — the reducer derives it; a component may not set it')
240	            continue
241	        if kl in VERBATIM_KEYS:
242	            continue                       # raw text from the world. It may say anything.
243	        _scan_value(str(k), v, bad)
244	    return bad
245	
246	
247	def _observations(events: list['Event']) -> list['Event']:
248	    """THE OBSERVATIONS ONLY. A reducer NEVER reads another reducer's recorded verdict.
249	
250	    `record_derivation()` writes audit artifacts into the same log, and the module docstring has
251	    always claimed "NO REDUCER READS THEM". That was true only BY ACCIDENT OF KIND CHOICE — nothing
252	    enforced it. It was one line from being false in the worst way: a derived record written with
253	    kind=CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED is picked up by `derive_content_profile`'s `for e in events` loop,
254	    which keeps the LAST match — so the reducer would have read ITS OWN PREVIOUS OUTPUT as if it were
255	    an observation of the bytes, and the label would have become a cache of itself. That is the cookie
256	    banner, again, from the inside.
257	
258	    So the invariant is now structural, in one place, and every derive_*() below goes through it.
259	    """
260	    return [e for e in events if 'derived_by' not in e.payload]
261	
262	
263	class LedgerCorrupt(Exception):
264	    """The persisted log does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never silently repaired."""
265	
266	
267	class Ledger:
268	    """Append-only, DURABLE, and RELOADABLE. You may add an observation. You may not edit, delete,
269	    or conclude.
270	
271	    IT DID NOT RELOAD. `__init__` set `self._events = []` and never read the file, so a ledger opened
272	    over an existing JSONL began with an EMPTY HISTORY — while still appending to the end of it. Every
273	    standalone script therefore reduced over the handful of events IT had emitted, not over what
274	    happened. `derive_route_status()` would find one adapter planned instead of four and call the route
275	    COMPLETE; `derive_coverage_status()` would license a scoped absence off a history it could not see.
276	    A reducer over an append-only log that does not read the log is not a reducer over anything — and
277	    it fails in the direction of CONFIDENCE, which is the only direction that ships.
278	    """
279	
280	    def __init__(self, path: Path | str | None = None, *, load: bool = True):
281	        self._events: list[Event] = []
282	        self._path = Path(path) if path else None
283	        self._seq = 0
284	        if self._path is not None:
285	            self._path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
286	            if load and self._path.exists():
287	                self._load()
288	
289	    @classmethod
290	    def load(cls, path: Path | str) -> 'Ledger':
291	        """Open the ledger AS IT STANDS ON DISK. This is the constructor a standalone script wants."""
292	        return cls(path, load=True)
293	
294	    # -- read the history back --------------------------------------------------------------------
295	    def _load(self) -> None:
296	        """A PERSISTED LEDGER IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT, and it is exactly as untrusted as the corpus was.
297	
298	        The emit-time guard is RE-APPLIED here. Bypassing it on read would leave the door it locks
299	        standing open: `echo '{"payload":{"content_status":"FULLTEXT"}}' >> ledger.jsonl` is not a
300	        harder attack than calling emit(), it is an easier one.
301	
302	        A record written by a reducer (it carries `derived_by`) is exempt from that scan, because its
303	        whole job is to hold a derived verdict. Forging one buys nothing: NO REDUCER READS THEM. They
304	        are audit artifacts, and every derive_*() in this file re-derives from the OBSERVATIONS. That
305	        is the difference between a ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped
306	        FULLTEXT on a cookie banner.
307	        """
308	        seen: set[int] = set()
309	        events: list[Event] = []
310	        for i, line in enumerate(self._path.read_text().splitlines(), 1):
311	            line = line.strip()
312	            if not line:
313	                continue
314	            where = f'{self._path.name}:{i}'
315	            try:
316	                d = json.loads(line)
317	            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
318	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: not JSON — {e}')
319	            missing = {'seq', 'unit', 'kind', 'actor', 'payload', 'ts'} - set(d)
320	            if missing:
321	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: missing field(s) {sorted(missing)}')
322	            if d['kind'] not in EventKind.ALL:
323	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: unknown event kind {d["kind"]!r}')
324	            if not isinstance(d['payload'], dict):
325	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: payload is not an object')
326	            if not isinstance(d['seq'], int) or d['seq'] in seen:
327	                raise LedgerCorrupt(f'{where}: seq {d["seq"]!r} is duplicated or not an integer — an '
328	                                    f'append-only log with a repeated sequence number has been edited')
329	            if 'derived_by' not in d['payload']:
330	                problems = _scan_for_conclusions(d['kind'], d['payload'])
331	                if problems:
332	                    raise LedgerCorrupt(
333	                        f'{where}: this line writes a CONCLUSION into the ledger — the emit guard '
334	                        f'refuses it, and so does the loader:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
335	            seen.add(d['seq'])
336	            events.append(Event(seq=d['seq'], unit=d['unit'], kind=d['kind'], actor=d['actor'],
337	                                payload=d['payload'], ts=d['ts']))
338	        events.sort(key=lambda e: e.seq)
339	        self._events = events
340	        self._seq = max(seen, default=0)     # ...so the next append CONTINUES the log, never collides
341	
342	    # -- write side: exactly one method, and it is guarded -------------------------------------
343	    def emit(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, **payload) -> Event:
344	        if kind not in EventKind.ALL:
345	            raise ForbiddenLabel(f'unknown event kind {kind!r}')
346	        problems = _scan_for_conclusions(kind, payload)
347	        if problems:
348	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
349	                f'{actor} tried to write a CONCLUSION into the ledger:\n    ' + '\n    '.join(problems))
350	        return self._append(unit, kind, actor, payload)
351	
352	    def _append(self, unit: str, kind: str, actor: str, payload: dict) -> Event:
353	        self._seq += 1
354	        ev = Event(seq=self._seq, unit=unit, kind=kind, actor=actor, payload=dict(payload))
355	        self._events.append(ev)
356	        if self._path:
357	            # One line, one open, closed immediately: the record is with the OS before emit() returns.
358	            # An event that is in memory but not on disk is a history that disagrees with itself the
359	            # moment the process dies.
360	            with open(self._path, 'a') as fh:
361	                fh.write(ev.to_json() + '\n')
362	                fh.flush()
363	        return ev
364	
365	    # -- the reducer's audit trail --------------------------------------------------------------
366	    def record_derivation(self, unit: str, kind: str, reducer: str, inputs: list[int],
367	                          **verdict) -> Event:
368	        """A reducer may append its own verdict — FOR AUDIT ONLY. Nobody else can call this.
369	
370	        The frame check below is not decoration. `emit()` is the door components use, and it refuses
371	        conclusions. This is the door the reducer uses, and it is bolted from the inside: if the
372	        calling frame is not this module, it raises. There is no third door.
373	
374	        Downstream reducers NEVER TRUST these records — they RE-DERIVE from the observations. A
375	        recorded verdict is an audit artifact, not an authority. That is the difference between a
376	        ledger and a cache, and caching the label is how we shipped "FULLTEXT" on a cookie banner.
377	        """
378	        import inspect
379	        caller = inspect.currentframe().f_back
380	        if caller.f_globals.get('__name__') != __name__:
381	            raise ForbiddenLabel(
382	                f'{caller.f_globals.get("__name__")!r} tried to record a DERIVED label directly. '
383	                f'Only a reducer inside {__name__} may. Emit OBSERVATIONS and call derive_*().')
384	        return self._append(unit, kind, reducer,
385	                            {**verdict, 'derived_by': reducer, 'from_events': list(inputs)})
386	
387	    # -- read side ------------------------------------------------------------------------------
388	    def events(self, unit: str | None = None, kind: str | None = None) -> list[Event]:
389	        return [e for e in self._events
390	                if (unit is None or e.unit == unit) and (kind is None or e.kind == kind)]
391	
392	    def units(self) -> list[str]:
393	        return list(dict.fromkeys(e.unit for e in self._events))
394	
395	    def __len__(self) -> int:
396	        return len(self._events)
397	
398	
399	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
400	# OBSERVERS — pure functions from raw content to OBSERVATIONS. They conclude nothing.
401	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
402	
403	_CID = re.compile(r'\(cid:\d+\)')
404	_CHROME = re.compile(
405	    r'website uses cookies|accept\s+(?:all\s+)?cookies|by clicking the "accept" button|'
406	    r'enable javascript|sign in to (?:your account|continue)|create an account|'
407	    r'this site requires|subscribe to (?:read|view)|purchase access|add to cart|'
408	    # ...the repository LANDING PAGES that fooled us: Oxford ORA, PubMed Central, generic portals.
409	    r'skip to main content|we welcome feedback|send message|research archive|'
410	    r'privacy policy|terms of use|all rights reserved|official websites use',
411	    re.I)
412	_PUBLISHED_STAMP = re.compile(r'published version|version of record|\(refereed\)|original citation', re.I)
413	
414	#: ══ THE ACCEPTED-MANUSCRIPT STAMP — THE V9 P0'S FIFTH HOP, AND ITS SEVENTH ══════════════════════════
415	#:
416	#: `_PUBLISHED_STAMP` above matches the phrase "published version". A repository cover sheet prints that
417	#: phrase IN TWO OPPOSITE SENSES, and until an accepted-manuscript stamp existed the reducer could not
418	#: tell them apart:
419	#:
420	#:    LSE, on the file that IS the AER article:
421	#:        "Article (Published version) (Refereed)"        <- the library says: THIS FILE is the VoR
422	#:    White Rose, on an author's manuscript:
423	#:        "This is an author produced version of a paper published in <journal>.
424	#:         Citation for the published version: ..."       <- the library says: THE VoR IS ELSEWHERE
425	#:
426	#: The second one is a cover sheet POINTING AWAY FROM ITSELF, and `_PUBLISHED_STAMP` read it as the
427	#: document testifying that it IS the version of record. `derive_semantic_binding` returned
428	#: VERSION_PUBLISHED, `derive_eligibility` returned ADMISSIBLE, and an accepted manuscript became
429	#: journal evidence — the SAME P0 as the census ladder, in a different file, reached by a different road.
430	#:
431	#: `_ACCEPTED_STAMP` IS NO LONGER DEFINED HERE. It WAS — a second, hand-maintained copy of the same
432	#: pattern that lives in provenance._AM_MARK — and the two DIVERGED, which is the seventh hop: hop 6
433	#: widened `_AM_MARK` to catch the NIH author-manuscript tells ("author manuscript; available in PMC",
434	#: "NIHMS<digits>") and this copy never learned them, so `derive_semantic_binding` scored an NIH
435	#: accepted manuscript VERSION_PUBLISHED while provenance ruled it `accepted_manuscript`. Two detectors
436	#: for ONE question ("which stamps mark an accepted manuscript") is two answers waiting to disagree, and
437	#: the one that drifts is always the one a gate reads. So the name now BINDS THE IMPORTED `_AM_MARK`
438	#: (see the import at the top of this file): ONE statement, ONE place, and the two lanes cannot drift.
439	#: PREPRINT / WORKING-PAPER STAMPS — a DATA-DRIVEN REGISTRY, not a wall of ifs. Each row is
440	#: (repository/series key, header-regex fragment). ADDING A NEW REPOSITORY IS A ONE-LINE DATA EDIT.
441	#:
442	#: A stamp in a document's OWN header is evidence the manifestation is a WORKING-PAPER / PREPRINT
443	#: EXPRESSION — NOT the journal version of record. Peer review CHANGES NUMBERS (Acemoglu & Restrepo's
444	#: robot effect: 0.37pp in the NBER working paper, 0.2pp in the published JPE), so under a journal-only
445	#: policy a stamped manifestation is a DISCOVERY LEAD, never journal evidence (derive_semantic_binding
446	#: -> VERSION_PREPRINT -> derive_eligibility -> DISCOVERY_LEAD).
447	#:
448	#: WHY THE LIST GREW: the census caught the reducer scoring a GLO Discussion Paper and an arXiv preprint
449	#: AS JOURNAL FULLTEXT — the P0 crawling back through a gap. The old pattern knew nber|iza|arxiv but NOT
450	#: the working-paper vocabulary below, so an EconStor working paper (Gray 2013) passed as the journal
451	#: article. Whenever a new repository stamps its cover page, ADD A ROW HERE — do not touch a reducer.
452	PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY: list[tuple[str, str]] = [
453	    #  registry key           header pattern (case-insensitive; matched as a fragment)
454	    ('nber',              r'nber working paper|national bureau of economic research'),
455	    ('iza',               r'iza\s+(?:dp|discussion paper)'),
456	    ('glo',               r'glo discussion paper'),
457	    ('cepr',              r'cepr discussion paper'),
458	    ('ssrn',              r'\bssrn\b'),
459	    ('repec',             r'\brepec\b'),
460	    ('mpra',              r'\bmpra\b'),
461	    ('munich_repec',      r'munich personal repec'),
462	    ('econstor',          r'\beconstor\b'),
463	    ('documento_trabajo', r'documento de trabajo'),
464	    ('cahier_recherche',  r'cahier de recherche'),
465	    ('discussion_paper',  r'discussion paper no'),
466	    ('working_paper',     r'working paper'),
467	    ('arxiv',             r'arxiv:\s*\d'),
468	    ('preprint',          r'\bpreprint\b'),
469	    ('this_version',      r'this\s+(?:draft|version):'),
470	    ('preliminary',       r'preliminary(?: and incomplete)?|do not (?:cite|quote) without'),
471	]
472	
473	#: The registry compiled to ONE alternation. Named groups let a self-test report WHICH stamp fired
474	#: (see preprint_stamp_key). observe_text scans the HEADER ONLY: every economics paper CITES NBER
475	#: working papers in its REFERENCES, and a whole-text scan reads the bibliography, not the paper —
476	#: which is exactly how a naive scan once called the actual JEP article a working paper.
477	_PREPRINT_STAMP = re.compile(
478	    '|'.join(f'(?P<{key}>{pat})' for key, pat in PREPRINT_STAMP_REGISTRY), re.I)
479	
480	
481	def preprint_stamp_key(text: str) -> str | None:
482	    """The registry key of the FIRST preprint/working-paper stamp in `text`, or None.
483	
484	    A pure OBSERVATION helper — it CONCLUDES NOTHING. derive_semantic_binding() decides what a stamp
485	    MEANS (a DIFFERENT SOURCE until version equivalence is proven); this only reports which one is
486	    present, for provenance and for the self-test that proves the registry covers each repository.
487	    """
488	    m = _PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)
489	    return m.lastgroup if m else None
490	
491	
492	#: A REPOSITORY COVER SHEET IS NOT THE ARTICLE'S FRONT MATTER (Sol V9 §5).
493	#:
494	#: An institutional repository staples its own page onto the front of the PDF: "This is a repository copy
495	#: of…", the handle, the licence, and — fatally — A FULL CITATION OF THE ARTICLE, DOI AND ALL. So the
496	#: first thing a front-matter DOI extractor sees is a DOI printed by the LIBRARY, not by the publisher,
497	#: and it is printed identically whether the file underneath is the VoR, the accepted manuscript, or
498	#: (this has happened) a DIFFERENT PAPER filed under the wrong handle. A cover sheet proves what a
499	#: repository BELIEVES it deposited. The article's own front matter proves what it IS.
500	#:
501	#: So the cover sheet is SEGMENTED OFF and the article's front matter is read after it. The cover sheet
502	#: is not discarded — it is a real observation, kept under `cover_sheet_text` — it is just not allowed
503	#: to be the article's own testimony about itself.
504	#: (the pattern itself now lives in provenance._COVER_SHEET and is imported at the top of this file —
505	#:  see the note there. It is the SAME cover sheet in both lanes, so it is described in ONE place.)
506	
507	_DOI_RE = re.compile(r'\b10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s"\'<>,;)\]]+', re.I)
508	
509	#: IS THERE A BYLINE AT ALL? Sol V9 §5 lets DIFFERENT_WORK be reached by "an incompatible foreign title
510	#: PLUS A DISJOINT BYLINE" — and BOTH halves are load-bearing. A byline that is ABSENT is not a byline
511	#: that is disjoint, and the difference is the whole of this project's disease.
512	#:
513	#: This was not academic. My first cut of the rule read "no requested author appears in the front matter"
514	#: as disjointness, and the router's own demo convicted it INSIDE A MINUTE: the cookie-banner fixture —
515	#: "This website uses cookies. Sign in to continue." — came out DIFFERENT_WORK. A landing page names no
516	#: author because IT IS NOT A DOCUMENT, and reading that silence as "somebody else's paper" would have
517	#: quarantined it under a claim about authorship that nothing supported. It is a landing page. Say that.
518	#:
519	#: So a byline must be POSITIVELY OBSERVED: an explicit authorship cue, or two full names conjoined. When
520	#: this is False the reducer may not use disjointness for anything, in either direction.
521	#: An explicit authorship CUE. Case-insensitive: "By Yang-Hui He", "AUTHORS:", "Author —".
522	_BYLINE_CUE = re.compile(r'\bby\s+[A-Z]|\bauthors?\s*[:—-]', re.I)
523	#: ...or a conjoined pair of full names. CASE-SENSITIVE ON PURPOSE — names are capitalised, and folding
524	#: case here would match "the paper and the data", which is not a byline.
525	_BYLINE = re.compile(r'[A-Z][a-z]+\s+[A-Z][\w.-]+\s*(?:,|\band\b)\s*[A-Z][a-z]+\s+[A-Z][\w.-]+')
526	
527	
528	def segment_front_matter(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> tuple[str, str]:
529	    """-> (cover_sheet_text, article_front_matter). Sol V9 §5: "Repository cover sheets are SEGMENTED
530	    from article front matter."
531	
532	    The boundary is taken at the first form-feed after the last cover-sheet marker (a PDF page break —
533	    the cover sheet IS a separate page, which is the one structural thing that is reliably true of it),
534	    and failing that at the end of the marker block. If no cover-sheet marker is present, the cover sheet
535	    is empty and the article's front matter is simply the header — which is the common case and must
536	    stay cheap.
537	    """
538	    head = text[:header_chars * 4]           # look further than the header: a cover sheet displaces it
539	    hits = list(_COVER_SHEET.finditer(head))
540	    if not hits:
541	        return '', text[:header_chars]
542	    end = hits[-1].end()
543	    ff = text.find('\x0c', end)
544	    boundary = ff + 1 if 0 <= ff < len(head) else end
545	    return text[:boundary], text[boundary:boundary + header_chars]
546	
547	
548	def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
549	    """Everything the ledger needs to know about a blob of retrieved text — and NOT ONE CONCLUSION.
550	
551	    Note `header_chars`: a preprint stamp is only evidence of what a document IS when it appears in
552	    the document's OWN header. Every economics paper cites NBER working papers in its REFERENCES —
553	    scanning the whole text for "NBER Working Paper" identifies the bibliography, not the paper.
554	    (That naive scan called the actual Journal of Economic Perspectives article a working paper.)
555	    """
556	    words = text.split()
557	    header = text[:header_chars]
558	    # THE ARTICLE'S OWN FRONT MATTER, with any repository cover sheet segmented off it. The DOIs found
559	    # HERE are the document's own claim about which article it is — as distinct from the DOI a library
560	    # printed on a cover page, which is the library's claim about what it filed.
561	    cover, article_front = segment_front_matter(text, header_chars)
562	    front_dois = sorted({d.rstrip('.').lower() for d in _DOI_RE.findall(article_front)})
563	    cid_tokens = sum(1 for t in words if 'cid:' in t)
564	    stripped = _CID.sub('', text)
565	    real_words = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', stripped))
566	    # How much REAL text is in the header? A title page of pure (cid:NN) glyph codes decodes to
567	    # nothing — and a header we cannot READ is a header we cannot CHECK AN IDENTITY AGAINST. It is
568	    # not a stranger's paper. It is an unreadable one, and those are different facts.
569	    header_real = len(re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]{3,}', _CID.sub('', header)))
570	    return {
571	        'word_count': len(words),
572	        'char_count': len(text),
573	        'readable_word_count': real_words,
574	        'glyph_garbage_ratio': round(cid_tokens / max(1, len(words)), 4),
575	        'chrome_markers': len(_CHROME.findall(text)),
576	        # ---- VERSION FURNITURE, READ IN THE RIGHT VOICE ----------------------------------------
577	        # A stamp means something different depending on WHO PRINTED IT. The article's own front
578	        # matter is the DOCUMENT'S TESTIMONY ABOUT ITSELF; the cover sheet is THE LIBRARY'S OPINION OF
579	        # WHAT IT FILED. These were previously read from the same undifferentiated `header` slice, so a
580	        # library's "citation for the published version" was scored as the article calling itself the
581	        # version of record. They are separated now, and `derive_semantic_binding` weighs them by voice.
582	        'has_cover_sheet': bool(cover),
583	        'published_stamp_in_header': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
584	        'published_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PUBLISHED_STAMP.search(cover)),
585	        'accepted_stamp_in_header': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(article_front)),
586	        'accepted_stamp_in_cover': bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(cover)),
587	        'preprint_stamp_in_header': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(article_front)),
588	        'preprint_stamp_in_cover': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(cover)),
589	        'preprint_stamp_anywhere': bool(_PREPRINT_STAMP.search(text)),
590	        'header_real_words': header_real,
591	        # the window identity is checked in. BOUNDED ON PURPOSE: searching the WHOLE document for an
592	        # author name matches the REFERENCES — every requested author is "present" somewhere in every
593	        # paper. (A mathematician named Parry is cited in the maths paper we misfiled under Parry
594	        # et al. A whole-text search "confirmed" the wrong paper.)
595	        'identity_window': header,
596	        'header_text': header[:400],
597	        # ---- SOL V9 §5: IDENTITY FROM THE FETCHED CONTENT ITSELF -------------------------------
598	        # The DOI the ARTICLE prints on itself (JATS article-DOI, PDF front matter, HTML header) — with
599	        # any repository COVER SHEET segmented off, because a cover sheet's DOI is the library's opinion
600	        # of what it deposited, not the document's testimony about what it is.
601	        'front_matter_dois': front_dois,
602	        'cover_sheet_chars': len(cover),
603	        'cover_sheet_text': cover[:400],
604	        'article_front_matter': article_front[:400],
605	        # A BYLINE IS PRESENT — or it is not. `False` means the reducer may not reason about
606	        # disjointness AT ALL: an absent byline is not a disjoint one. (A cookie banner names no author
607	        # because it is not a document, not because a stranger wrote it.)
608	        'byline_present': bool(_BYLINE_CUE.search(article_front) or _BYLINE.search(article_front)),
609	    }
610	
611	
612	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
613	# REDUCERS — the ONLY place a label may come into existence.
614	# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
615	
616	# ---- 1. backend outcome ----------------------------------------------------------------------
617	
618	RESPONDED            = 'RESPONDED'                    # the backend answered with content
619	NOT_INDEXED          = 'NOT_INDEXED_BY_THIS_BACKEND'  # 404 — a fact about THEIR INDEX, not the world
620	BACKEND_FAILED       = 'BACKEND_FAILED'               # 429/503/timeout — a fact about OUR REQUEST
621	ACCESS_BLOCKED       = 'ACCESS_BLOCKED'               # 401/403/paywall — a fact about ENTITLEMENT
622	NO_OUTCOME           = 'NO_OUTCOME'                   # attempted, never came back. A HANG, not a gap.
623	
624	
625	#: The four kinds that constitute A REQUEST. CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED and MANIFESTATION_FETCHED also carry
626	#: an `adapter` (for provenance — WHICH backend found this), but they are not request outcomes and must
627	#: not be read as one.
628	_REQUEST_KINDS = (EventKind.BACKEND_ATTEMPTED, EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED,
629	                  EventKind.THROTTLED, EventKind.BLOCKED)
630	
631	#: Worst-first. An adapter that hung on one request and answered another has NOT answered: we asked it
632	#: something and never found out. Between RESPONDED and NOT_INDEXED, RESPONDED wins — a backend that
633	#: answered "not in my index" for one query and returned a paper for another IS WORKING.
634	_OUTCOME_PRECEDENCE = (NO_OUTCOME, BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED, RESPONDED, NOT_INDEXED)
635	
636	
637	def _outcome_of_request(evs: list[Event]) -> str:
638	    """The outcome of ONE logical request, read from its FINAL terminal event.
639	
640	    A retry loop emits BACKEND_ATTEMPTED + one terminal event PER ATTEMPT, all sharing a `request_id`.
641	    So a request reads:  ATTEMPTED, THROTTLED, ATTEMPTED, THROTTLED, ATTEMPTED, RESPONSE_RECEIVED(200)
642	
643	    The 429s ARE NOT DELETED — they are on disk forever, and `Ledger` has no operation that could
644	    remove them. But a throttle the backoff RESOLVED and a throttle we GAVE UP ON are different facts
645	    about the world, and only the second one means we never got the answer. Reading "any 429 anywhere"
646	    as BACKEND_FAILED would mark every route degraded the moment a single retry succeeded, and a route
647	    that is always degraded can never license a scoped absence — which sounds conservative and is
648	    actually just as blind as always calling it complete.
649	
650	        exhausted retries -> final terminal event is THROTTLED         -> BACKEND_FAILED. FOREVER.
651	        429 then 200      -> final terminal event is RESPONSE_RECEIVED -> RESPONDED.
652	    """
653	    terminal = [e for e in evs if e.kind in (EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED, EventKind.THROTTLED,
654	                                             EventKind.BLOCKED)]
655	    if not terminal:
656	        return NO_OUTCOME               # attempted and never came back. A HANG, not a gap.
657	    e = terminal[-1]
658	    if e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED:
659	        return BACKEND_FAILED
660	    if e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED:
661	        return ACCESS_BLOCKED
662	    code = e.payload.get('http_status')
663	    if code in (429, 503):
664	        return BACKEND_FAILED           # even if someone logged it as a "response"
665	    if code in (401, 403):
666	        return ACCESS_BLOCKED
667	    if code == 404:
668	        return NOT_INDEXED
669	    if e.payload.get('transport_error'):
670	        return BACKEND_FAILED           # timeout / DNS / reset / bad JSON / 5xx
671	    return RESPONDED
672	
673	
674	# ---- 1b. ROUTE LINEAGE — WHOSE DOCUMENT IS THIS? (Sol V9 §1) ---------------------------------
675	#
676	# THE BUG THIS EXISTS TO CLOSE: a route could be credited with a document some OTHER route fetched.
677	#
678	# The chain is:  resolver request -> candidate -> content request -> redirects -> manifestation
679	# and `candidate_id` is the thread that runs through all of it. A MANIFESTATION_FETCHED event carries the
680	# CONTENT HOST in `adapter` (`content:arxiv.org`) — never the resolver that proposed the URL — so
681	# "which adapter found this document?" is NOT answerable from the manifestation alone. It is answerable
682	# only by walking back to the CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED event that minted the candidate_id, whose `adapter`
683	# IS the resolver.
684	#
685	# Reducing over "all manifestations of the unit", as the discovery reducer did, answers a different
686	# question — "did ANYBODY get a document for this work?" — and reports it once per route. Every route
687	# then inherits every other route's success, unique incremental yield is identically equal to gross
688	# yield, and the ladder Sol asks for in §9 measures nothing at all.
689	
690	
691	def candidates_of_adapter(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> set[str]:
692	    """The candidate_ids THIS ADAPTER PROPOSED. The lineage root — an adapter owns what it discovered."""
693	    return {str(e.payload['candidate_id']) for e in _observations(events)
694	            if e.kind == EventKind.CANDIDATE_IDENTIFIED
695	            and e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter
696	            and e.payload.get('candidate_id')}
697	
698	
699	def manifestations_of_adapter(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> list[Event]:
700	    """The MANIFESTATION_FETCHED events that descend from a candidate `adapter` proposed.
701	
702	    Also includes manifestations whose OWN adapter is `adapter` — a content host IS a route (if nber.org
703	    403s us we were blocked BY NBER), and when a fetcher pulls a URL with no candidate lineage the
704	    content host is the only route there is.
705	
706	    A manifestation with NO candidate_id and a different adapter belongs to NOBODY, and is therefore
707	    credited to nobody. That is the safe default and the honest one: we do not know which route earned
708	    it, and inventing an answer is the failure this reducer exists to end.
709	    """
710	    mine = candidates_of_adapter(events, adapter)
711	    out = []
712	    for e in _observations(events):
713	        if e.kind != EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED:
714	            continue
715	        cid = str(e.payload.get('candidate_id') or '')
716	        if (cid and cid in mine) or e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter:
717	            out.append(e)
718	    return out
719	
720	
721	def events_of_adapter_lineage(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> list[Event]:
722	    """This unit's events with EVERY OTHER ROUTE'S DOCUMENTS REMOVED — and their profiles with them.
723	
724	    This is what lets the existing content reducers (`derive_content_profile`, `derive_semantic_binding`)
725	    answer "what did THIS ROUTE get?" without any of them learning a second vocabulary. They still reduce
726	    over an event list; the list is just no longer somebody else's.
727	
728	    MANIFESTATION_FETCHED and its CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED are emitted ADJACENTLY and are paired by
729	    adjacency downstream (`_holdings`), so a foreign manifestation must take its profile out with it.
730	    Dropping the manifestation alone would leave an orphan profile that pairs with the NEXT
731	    manifestation — one route's bytes wearing another route's word count, which is the same class of bug
732	    one layer down.
733	    """
734	    keep = {id(e) for e in manifestations_of_adapter(events, adapter)}
735	    out: list[Event] = []
736	    drop_profile = False
737	    for e in events:
738	        if e.kind == EventKind.MANIFESTATION_FETCHED and 'derived_by' not in e.payload:
739	            drop_profile = id(e) not in keep
740	            if not drop_profile:
741	                out.append(e)
742	            continue
743	        if e.kind == EventKind.CONTENT_PROFILE_DERIVED and drop_profile:
744	            drop_profile = False
745	            continue
746	        drop_profile = False
747	        out.append(e)
748	    return out
749	
750	
751	def derive_backend_outcome(events: list[Event], adapter: str) -> str:
752	    """What actually happened to one adapter. `None` is not an answer — it is four different worlds.
753	
754	    An adapter may be asked more than once (four candidate PDFs from one content host; a DOI lookup and
755	    a title search). Each logical request is scored on its own; the adapter reports its WORST.
756	    """
757	    mine = [e for e in _observations(events)
758	            if e.payload.get('adapter') == adapter and e.kind in _REQUEST_KINDS]
759	    if not mine:
760	        return NO_OUTCOME
761	
762	    # Events carrying no `request_id` are a HISTORY WRITTEN BEFORE RETRIES EXISTED (our replay, the
763	    # hand-written invariants, any older log). They are one bucket, scored by the original rule: ANY
764	    # throttle anywhere condemns the adapter. Back-compat is not politeness here — a reducer that
765	    # changed its answer about a log it had already reduced would make the whole record unreadable.
766	    buckets: dict[str, list[Event]] = {}
767	    for e in mine:
768	        buckets.setdefault(str(e.payload.get('request_id') or ''), []).append(e)
769	
770	    outcomes: list[str] = []
771	    for rid, evs in buckets.items():
772	        if rid == '':
773	            if any(e.kind == EventKind.THROTTLED for e in evs):
774	                outcomes.append(BACKEND_FAILED)
775	            elif any(e.kind == EventKind.BLOCKED for e in evs):
776	                outcomes.append(ACCESS_BLOCKED)
777	            else:
778	                first = [e for e in evs if e.kind == EventKind.RESPONSE_RECEIVED][:1]
779	                outcomes.append(_outcome_of_request(first) if first else NO_OUTCOME)
780	        else:
781	            outcomes.append(_outcome_of_request(evs))
782	
783	    for o in _OUTCOME_PRECEDENCE:
784	        if o in outcomes:
785	            return o
786	    return NO_OUTCOME
787	
788	
789	# ---- 2. route status -------------------------------------------------------------------------
790	
791	@dataclass(frozen=True)
792	class RouteStatus:
793	    state: str                     # UNROUTED | INCOMPLETE | COMPLETE_DEGRADED | COMPLETE
794	    planned: tuple[str, ...]
795	    outcomes: dict[str, str]
796	    missing: tuple[str, ...]       # planned adapters with NO terminal outcome
797	    failed: tuple[str, ...]        # adapters that were throttled/blocked/errored
798	    budget_stopped: bool
799	
800	    @property
801	    def supports_absence(self) -> bool:
802	        """THE question. Only an ADEQUATE route licenses "we looked, and it is not there."
803	
804	        Every planned adapter must have genuinely ANSWERED. One 429, one hang, or a budget stop and
805	        the honest label is SEARCH_FAILED — we do not know, and we may not say we do.
806	        """
807	        return (self.state == 'COMPLETE'
808	                and not self.missing and not self.failed and not self.budget_stopped
809	                and bool(self.planned))
810	
811	
812	def derive_route_status(events: list[Event]) -> RouteStatus:
813	    """route_complete means EVERY PLANNED ADAPTER HAS A TERMINAL OUTCOME RECORD.
814	
815	    It NEVER means "an adapter was mapped".
816	    """
817	    events = _observations(events)
818	    planned: list[str] = []
819	    for e in events:
820	        if e.kind == EventKind.ROUTE_PLANNED:
821	            planned += [a for a in e.payload.get('adapters', []) if a not in planned]
822	    if not planned:
823	        return RouteStatus('UNROUTED', (), {}, (), (), False)
824	
825	    budget = any(e.kind == EventKind.BUDGET_STOPPED for e in events)
826	    outcomes = {a: derive_backend_outcome(events, a) for a in planned}
827	    missing = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o == NO_OUTCOME)
828	
829	    # ── A BLOCKED RETRIEVAL IS A FAILED ROUTE. ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
830	    # The SEARCH adapters are the ones in ROUTE_PLANNED. But finding the document's address is not
831	    # holding the document: after a search answers, we go to the CONTENT HOST for the bytes, and that
832	    # host is a backend too — it can throttle us, and it can shut the door in our face.
833	    #
834	    # This was live, and it was the whole disease wearing a new coat. Autor (2015), JEP:
835	    #
836	    #     crossref                RESPONDED        (the metadata)
837	    #     unpaywall               RESPONDED        (...and here is where the PDF lives)
838	    #     content:www.aeaweb.org  ACCESS_BLOCKED   HTTP 403
839	    #
840	    # and because the content host was not a PLANNED adapter, the route read COMPLETE, `supports_
841	    # absence` read TRUE, and coverage reduced to SEARCHED_NONE — "a SCOPED ABSENCE, and the only
842	    # state that licenses one". A 403 FROM THE PUBLISHER had become a statement that the literature
843	    # is silent. That is a fact about ENTITLEMENT, printed as a fact about the world: the identical
844	    # error to reading a 429 as "no free copy exists", committed one layer down, by the cure.
845	    #
846	    # So EVERY adapter that failed counts against the route — planned or not. We do not get to say we
847	    # looked and found nothing when we were shown the door.
848	    all_adapters = {e.payload.get('adapter') for e in events
849	                    if e.kind in _REQUEST_KINDS and e.payload.get('adapter')}
850	    for a in sorted(all_adapters - set(planned)):
851	        o = derive_backend_outcome(events, a)
852	        if o in (BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED):
853	            outcomes[a] = o
854	    failed = tuple(a for a, o in outcomes.items() if o in (BACKEND_FAILED, ACCESS_BLOCKED))
855	
856	    if missing:
857	        state = 'INCOMPLETE'
858	    elif failed or budget:
859	        state = 'COMPLETE_DEGRADED'     # every adapter has a record — but the search DID NOT WORK
860	    else:
861	        state = 'COMPLETE'
862	    return RouteStatus(state, tuple(planned), outcomes, missing, failed, budget)
863

1061	def derive_content_profile(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
1062	    """FULLTEXT IS EARNED — AGAINST THE PROFILE FOR ITS KIND. It is never a thing a fetcher declares,
1063	    and it is never a number this module keeps to itself."""
1064	    best = _best_holding(events)
1065	    if best is None:
1066	        return C_CITATION, {'reason': 'no content was ever profiled'}
1067	    _manif, _prof, cls, info = best
1068	    n = len(_holdings(events))
1069	    if n > 1:
1070	        info = {**info, 'n_documents_held': n,
1071	                'reason': f'{info["reason"]} (the best of {n} documents held for this work)'}
1072	    return cls, info

1075	# ---- 4. semantic binding ---------------------------------------------------------------------
1076	
1077	SAME_WORK        = 'SAME_WORK'              # this IS the journal article
1078	VERSION_PUBLISHED = 'VERSION_OF_PUBLISHED'  # a repository copy OF the published version
1079	VERSION_ACCEPTED = 'VERSION_OF_ACCEPTED'    # an ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT. Peer review is not the last edit.
1080	VERSION_PREPRINT = 'VERSION_OF_PREPRINT'    # a working paper. A DIFFERENT SOURCE until proven equal.
1081	DIFFERENT_WORK   = 'DIFFERENT_WORK'         # we fetched somebody else's paper
1082	UNRESOLVED       = 'UNRESOLVED_BINDING'
1083	
1084	#: THE IDENTITY ALLOWLIST (Sol, binding-gate design §1). A span may be ATTRIBUTED only when the bytes'
1085	#: identity is POSITIVELY PROVEN to be the requested Work. This is an ALLOWLIST, not a denylist: a
1086	#: missing, malformed, stale, or newly-introduced verdict is NOT in it and therefore REJECTS by default.
1087	#: DIFFERENT_WORK -> quarantine (a stranger's paper); UNRESOLVED_BINDING -> lead-only (we cannot tell);
1088	#: both are `admitted=False`. Version disposition (which EXPRESSION, under which policy) is orthogonal
1089	#: and decided downstream — identity only says "these bytes ARE this Work".
1090	IDENTITY_PROVEN = frozenset({SAME_WORK, VERSION_PUBLISHED, VERSION_ACCEPTED, VERSION_PREPRINT})
1091	
1092	
1093	#: ══ WHICH EXPRESSION DO THESE BYTES TESTIFY TO BEING? ════════════════════════════════════════════
1094	#:
1095	#: THE ORDER OF THIS TUPLE IS THE RULE, AND IT IS A STATEMENT — not an accident of where an `if` landed
1096	#: in a ladder. The INADMISSIBLE kinds are asked FIRST, on purpose, because the commonest shape in an
1097	#: institutional repository carries BOTH kinds of stamp at once:
1098	#:
1099	#:     "This is an author produced version of a paper published in <journal>.     <- ACCEPTED
1100	#:      Citation for the published version: ..."                                  <- PUBLISHED
1101	#:
1102	#: The old reducer asked `published_stamp_in_header` first and answered VERSION_PUBLISHED. Reading the
1103	#: SECOND line and ignoring the FIRST is how an accepted manuscript became journal evidence. Whichever
1104	#: way a future edit reorders the code, the precedence lives HERE, in data, and `derive_eligibility`
1105	#: re-checks the result against a veto that does not consult this order at all.
1106	#:
1107	#: (`*_in_cover` is the LIBRARY's voice, `*_in_header` the DOCUMENT's own. For the two inadmissible
1108	#: kinds EITHER voice convicts — a repository saying "this is the author's manuscript" is describing
1109	#: the file it filed. For PUBLISHED, either voice may acquit, but only because nothing above it fired.)
1110	VERSION_EVIDENCE: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...], str], ...] = (
1111	    (VERSION_ACCEPTED,  ('accepted_stamp_in_cover', 'accepted_stamp_in_header'),
1112	     'the bytes (or the repository that filed them) declare this an ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT — acceptance '
1113	     'precedes copy-editing, proofs and the editor\'s last round, and THE NUMBERS MOVE ACROSS THEM'),
1114	    (VERSION_PREPRINT,  ('preprint_stamp_in_cover', 'preprint_stamp_in_header'),
1115	     'the header carries a preprint/working-paper stamp — peer review CHANGES NUMBERS; this is a '
1116	     'DIFFERENT SOURCE until version equivalence is proven'),
1117	    (VERSION_PUBLISHED, ('published_stamp_in_cover', 'published_stamp_in_header'),
1118	     'a published-version/refereed stamp, and NO accepted-manuscript or preprint stamp contradicting it'),
1119	)

1122	def version_evidence(prof: dict) -> tuple[str, str]:
1123	    """-> (binding, why). The ONE place a stamp becomes a version. Consulted by nothing else."""
1124	    for binding, fields, why in VERSION_EVIDENCE:
1125	        if any(prof.get(f) for f in fields):
1126	            return binding, why
1127	    return SAME_WORK, 'no version furniture contradicts the identity we confirmed'

1130	def _norm(s: str) -> set[str]:
1131	    stop = {'the', 'a', 'an', 'of', 'and', 'for', 'in', 'on', 'to', 'from', 'with'}
1132	    return {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]+', (s or '').lower()) if w not in stop and len(w) > 2}

1135	def derive_semantic_binding(events: list[Event]) -> tuple[str, dict]:
1136	    """Is the text we fetched the work we asked for? A fetcher MAY NOT ANSWER THIS ABOUT ITSELF.
1137	
1138	    Sol: "A predecessor working paper and later journal article may be closely related, but they are
1139	    DIFFERENT SOURCES until version equivalence is proven."
1140	    """
1141	    # THE SAME DOCUMENT THE LABEL DESCRIBES. `_best_holding` returns a manifestation TOGETHER WITH ITS
1142	    # OWN profile — so identity is checked against the header of the very bytes whose completeness was
1143	    # judged. Reading "the last manifestation" and "the last profile" independently could pair one
1144	    # document's byline with another document's word count, and answer about a document that does not
1145	    # exist.
1146	    best = _best_holding(events)
1147	    if best is None:
1148	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': 'nothing was fetched'}
1149	    fetched, prof, _cls, _info = best
1150	    return derive_binding_core(
1151	        requested_doi=fetched.get('requested_doi', ''),
1152	        requested_title=fetched.get('requested_title', ''),
1153	        requested_authors=fetched.get('requested_authors', []),
1154	        prof=prof,
1155	        document_title=fetched.get('document_title', ''),
1156	        observed_byline=fetched.get('byline', ''))
1157	
1158	
1159	def derive_binding_core(*, requested_doi: str, requested_title: str,
1160	                        requested_authors: list, prof: dict,
1161	                        document_title: str = '', observed_byline: str = '') -> tuple[str, dict]:
1162	    """THE single per-manifestation identity rule. `derive_semantic_binding(events)` is a thin wrapper
1163	    that pulls the requested identity + profile off the ledger and calls this; `provenance.ingest_bytes`
1164	    calls it directly with the Work's identity and `observe_text(bytes)`. ONE reducer, both paths — a
1165	    second identity rule in the graph is exactly the split that let DIFFERENT_WORK bytes be admitted.
1166	
1167	    `prof` is an `observe_text()` result (or a stored profile with the same keys): it supplies
1168	    front_matter_dois, byline_present, header_real_words, identity_window/header_text, and the version
1169	    stamps. `requested_*` is what we ASKED for (the Work). Positive evidence only — DIFFERENT_WORK
1170	    needs a foreign DOI or a disjoint byline; absence is never a stranger's paper."""
1171	    # The identity window is BOUNDED (see observe_text): a whole-document scan finds every author in
1172	    # the references and "confirms" anything you ask it to.
1173	    window = document_title or prof.get('identity_window', '') \
1174	        or prof.get('header_text', '')
1175	    want_t = _norm(requested_title)
1176	    got_t = _norm(window)
1177	    want_a = {a.lower() for a in (requested_authors or [])}
1178	    byline = (observed_byline or window).lower()
1179	
1180	    overlap = len(want_t & got_t) / max(1, len(want_t))
1181	    author_hit = any(a in byline for a in want_a if a)
1182	    title_match = overlap >= 0.90
1183	    # A SPECIFIC title (>=4 content words) matching in full is itself strong identity evidence.
1184	    # A GENERIC one is not: {rise, machines} sits entirely inside {mathematics, rise, machines}.
1185	    title_specific = len(want_t) >= 4
1186	
1187	    # ── THE DOCUMENT'S OWN DOI (Sol V9 §5) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1188	    # Read from the ARTICLE'S front matter, with any repository cover sheet segmented off. This is the
1189	    # ONE identity signal that is both positive and decisive in BOTH directions: an exact front-matter
1190	    # DOI CONFIRMS, and a front matter that prints a DIFFERENT DOI and NOT ours is the positive evidence
1191	    # of a stranger's paper that DIFFERENT_WORK has always needed and never had.
1192	    want_doi = str(requested_doi or '').strip().lower().rstrip('.')
1193	    front_dois = [str(d).lower().rstrip('.') for d in (prof.get('front_matter_dois') or [])]
1194	    doi_hit = bool(want_doi and want_doi in front_dois)
1195	    foreign_doi = bool(want_doi and front_dois and not doi_hit)
1196	    #: A byline was POSITIVELY OBSERVED. Absence of one is NOT disjointness — see _BYLINE.
1197	    byline_present = bool(prof.get('byline_present'))
1198	
1199	    ev = {'title_overlap': round(overlap, 2), 'author_in_byline': author_hit,
1200	          'title_content_words': len(want_t),
1201	          'requested_title': (requested_title or '')[:70],
1202	          'front_matter_dois': front_dois[:4], 'doi_in_front_matter': doi_hit,
1203	          'cover_sheet_chars': prof.get('cover_sheet_chars', 0),
1204	          'header_text': (prof.get('header_text') or '')[:120]}
1205	
1206	    # ── AN UNREADABLE HEADER IS NOT A STRANGER'S PAPER. ───────────────────────────────────────
1207	    # Autor, Levy & Murnane (2003) arrives as a PDF whose title page is pure (cid:NN) glyph codes:
1208	    # ZERO readable words in the header, though the BODY has 10,902. An earlier version of this
1209	    # reducer read "no title, no author" as DIFFERENT_WORK — turning a FONT-ENCODING FAILURE into a
1210	    # confident claim about whose paper it is. That is this project's whole disease, committed by
1211	    # the cure. We cannot read it, therefore we cannot bind it. Say exactly that.
1212	    if prof.get('header_real_words', 0) < 10:
1213	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'the header decoded to {prof.get("header_real_words", 0)} '
1214	                                      f'readable words — we CANNOT READ it, so we cannot establish '
1215	                                      f'identity. Not a stranger\'s paper: an unreadable one', **ev}
1216	
1217	    # ── DIFFERENT_WORK NEEDS POSITIVE EVIDENCE OF A STRANGER. IT ALWAYS DID. ──────────────────
1218	    # Sol V9 §5 sets the bar exactly: DIFFERENT_WORK requires "a positive foreign DOI, or an
1219	    # incompatible foreign title PLUS a disjoint byline". And, verbatim: "A generic title without an
1220	    # author must NOT produce DIFFERENT_WORK; the current event reducer is TOO AGGRESSIVE here."
1221	    #
1222	    # It was. Two branches below used to return DIFFERENT_WORK on ABSENCE:
1223	    #
1224	    #   1. a generic-title match with no author in the header -> DIFFERENT_WORK ("title collision")
1225	    #   2. an author match with a weak title                  -> DIFFERENT_WORK ("same author, different
1226	    #                                                                             paper")
1227	    #
1228	    # Neither is positive evidence of anyone else's paper. (1) fires on a real article with a two-word
1229	    # title whose PDF header happens not to carry a byline — which describes a large fraction of
1230	    # publisher HTML. (2) fires on the RIGHT paper whose title page decoded badly, and it fires while the
1231	    # BYLINE AGREES WITH US, which is the opposite of a disjoint byline. Both convict on absence, and
1232	    # DIFFERENT_WORK is not a shrug — it is `wrong_work`, a QUARANTINE, and it destroys the document.
1233	    #
1234	    # UNRESOLVED is the honest verdict for both, and it costs us the same evidence while making a claim
1235	    # we can actually support. Absence of our evidence read as evidence of absence is the 429-means-"no
1236	    # free copy exists" error, and it does not become sound by being pointed at a PDF.
1237	    if foreign_doi:
1238	        # POSITIVE. The document PRINTS A DOI ON ITSELF, and it is not ours. (Cover sheet already
1239	        # segmented off — a library's citation of us is not the document identifying itself as us.)
1240	        return DIFFERENT_WORK, {
1241	            'reason': f'the article front matter prints DOI {front_dois[0]!r}, and we asked for '
1242	                      f'{want_doi!r}. The document says whose it is, and it is not ours', **ev}
1243	
1244	    # ── IDENTITY IS CONFIRMED BY ITS OWN DOI, AN AUTHOR, OR A SPECIFIC TITLE IN FULL. ─────────
1245	    confirmed = doi_hit or author_hit or (title_match and title_specific)
1246	
1247	    if not confirmed:
1248	        # POSITIVE, the second way: A DISJOINT BYLINE. The document SAYS WHO WROTE IT, we know who we
1249	        # asked for, and they are different people — while nothing else (its DOI, its title) puts our
1250	        # work in these bytes. That is testimony from the document, not an inference from silence.
1251	        #
1252	        # BOTH halves are required, and both are checked:
1253	        #   - `byline_present`: an authorship cue was POSITIVELY OBSERVED in the article's own front
1254	        #     matter (cover sheet segmented off). An ABSENT byline is not a disjoint one.
1255	        #   - `want_a`: we actually asked for named authors. If we asked for none, no byline on earth
1256	        #     can be disjoint from them, and claiming otherwise convicts every document we hold.
1257	        #
1258	        # THIS IS THE PARRY / YANG-HUI HE CASE, and it stays quarantined: we asked for Parry, the front
1259	        # matter says "By Yang-Hui He", and the only thing that "matched" was the two-word phrase "rise
1260	        # of the machines". What convicts it is the BYLINE — not the title collision, which convicts
1261	        # nothing.
1262	        if byline_present and want_a and not author_hit:
1263	            return DIFFERENT_WORK, {
1264	                'reason': f'the article front matter carries a byline, and it names NONE of the '
1265	                          f'{len(want_a)} requested author(s). Nothing else identifies these bytes as '
1266	                          f'ours (title overlap {overlap:.0%}, no matching front-matter DOI) — an '
1267	                          f'incompatible work with a DISJOINT BYLINE', **ev}
1268	        if title_match and not title_specific:
1269	            # A TITLE COLLISION, with NO byline to convict on. {rise, machines} sits inside
1270	            # {mathematics, rise, machines}. Sol V9 §5, verbatim: "A generic title without an author must
1271	            # NOT produce DIFFERENT_WORK." This is not knowledge that we hold a stranger's paper. It is
1272	            # knowledge that WE CANNOT TELL — and those are different facts, one of which is true.
1273	            return UNRESOLVED, {
1274	                'reason': f'the requested title has only {len(want_t)} content word(s) and no requested '
1275	                          f'author appears in the front matter. A {overlap:.0%} match on a GENERIC title '
1276	                          f'is a TITLE COLLISION — it establishes NEITHER identity NOR its absence. '
1277	                          f'UNRESOLVED, and therefore not attributable', **ev}
1278	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'title overlap {overlap:.0%}, no requested author in the '
1279	                                      f'header — identity is NOT established, so this text may not '
1280	                                      f'be attributed to this source', **ev}
1281	
1282	    # ...an author alone is not identity either: prolific authors write many papers. But the byline
1283	    # AGREES WITH US, so this is NOT a stranger's paper — it is a paper we cannot pin down. This branch
1284	    # used to return DIFFERENT_WORK ("same author, DIFFERENT PAPER") and it was the second half of Sol's
1285	    # "too aggressive" finding: it convicted while the byline was CONFIRMING us, which is the exact
1286	    # opposite of the disjoint byline the rule requires.
1287	    if author_hit and not doi_hit and not title_match and not title_specific:
1288	        return UNRESOLVED, {'reason': f'a requested author appears in the front matter but the title '
1289	                                      f'matches only {overlap:.0%} — same author, possibly a different '
1290	                                      f'paper. The byline is NOT disjoint, so this is not positive '
1291	                                      f'evidence of a stranger\'s work: it is UNRESOLVED', **ev}
1292	
1293	    # IDENTITY IS SETTLED — these bytes ARE the work we asked for. The remaining question is WHICH
1294	    # EXPRESSION of it, and that is decided in ONE place, by a declared precedence (VERSION_EVIDENCE),
1295	    # so that no reordering of this function can promote a manuscript to the version of record.
1296	    binding, why = version_evidence(prof)
1297	    if binding == SAME_WORK:
1298	        return SAME_WORK, {'reason': 'identity confirmed by '
1299	                                     + ('the article\'s OWN front-matter DOI' if doi_hit
1300	                                        else 'author and title' if author_hit and title_match
1301	                                        else 'author in the header' if author_hit
1302	                                        else f'a specific {len(want_t)}-word title matched in full')
1303	                                     + ', and no version furniture contradicting it', **ev}
1304	    return binding, {'reason': f'identity confirmed, but {why}', **ev}

1306	
1307	# ---- 5. eligibility --------------------------------------------------------------------------
1308	
1309	ADMISSIBLE     = 'ADMISSIBLE'
1310	DISCOVERY_LEAD = 'DISCOVERY_LEAD'     # Sol's word, exactly. Real text — but not attributable HERE.
1311	INADMISSIBLE   = 'INADMISSIBLE'
1312	
1313	#: ══ THE VERSION VETO (event lane) ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
1314	#: WHICH BINDINGS CAN NEVER BE JOURNAL EVIDENCE. A STATEMENT — so that adding a version kind without
1315	#: deciding its admissibility is impossible, and so that no branch can admit one by answering first.
1316	#: (`derive_eligibility` asserts on this AFTER it has chosen, which is the check that does not care
1317	#:  what order anything ran in.)
1318	NEVER_JOURNAL_EVIDENCE: dict[str, str] = {
1319	    VERSION_PREPRINT: ('working-paper text. A DISCOVERY LEAD, not automatically journal-attributable '
1320	                       'evidence — version equivalence is not proven'),
1321	    VERSION_ACCEPTED: ('accepted-manuscript text. Peer review is NOT the last thing that changes a '
1322	                       'number: copy-editing, proofs and the editor\'s final round come after it '
1323	                       '(Acemoglu & Restrepo, 0.37pp in the manuscript -> 0.2pp in the JPE). A '
1324	                       'DISCOVERY LEAD; a span reaches the journal only across a verified per-span '
1325	                       'correspondence into the VoR\'s own bytes'),
1326	}
1327	
1328	#: THE EXPRESSION KIND(S) a proven version binding can be cited AS. Eligibility tests membership of
1329	#: these against the policy's `permitted_expression_kinds`: a working-paper binding is admissible under
1330	#: ANY_VERSION (which permits `working_paper`/`preprint`) and a discovery lead under a policy that does
1331	#: not. This is registry data, keyed on the STRUCTURAL binding — no DOI, title, author or subject.
1332	_BINDING_EXPRESSION_KINDS: dict[str, tuple[str, ...]] = {
1333	    VERSION_PREPRINT: ('working_paper', 'preprint'),
1334	    VERSION_ACCEPTED: ('accepted_manuscript',),
1335	}
1336	
1337	
1338	def derive_eligibility(events: list[Event],
1339	                       policy: '_SourcePolicy' = _ANY_VERSION) -> tuple[str, dict]:
1340	    """May this text be attributed to THIS source, under THE REQUESTED SOURCE POLICY?
1341	
1342	    The policy is a provenance.SourcePolicy — the ADJUSTABLE source constraint the prompt imposed,
1343	    defaulting to ANY_VERSION. Version eligibility consults `policy.permitted_expression_kinds`: a
1344	    working-paper or accepted-manuscript binding is a DISCOVERY LEAD only when the policy in force does
1345	    NOT permit its expression kind (e.g. JOURNAL_ONLY). Under ANY_VERSION such a version is admissible
1346	    as its OWN expression — it is never journal evidence, but it is not unconditionally a lead either.
1347	    Citing the journal with a working paper's bytes remains FABRICATION under every policy.
1348	    """
1349	    cls, cinfo = derive_content_profile(events)
1350	    binding, binfo = derive_semantic_binding(events)
1351	
1352	    if cls == C_NOT_DOC:
1353	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'not a document ({cinfo["reason"]})',
1354	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1355	    if cls == C_UNREADABLE:
1356	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'unreadable ({cinfo["reason"]})',
1357	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1358	    if binding == DIFFERENT_WORK:
1359	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'wrong paper ({binfo["reason"]})',
1360	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1361	    # ---- THE VERSION VETO. A PRECONDITION, checked before anything may be admitted. -------------
1362	    # A preprint/accepted-manuscript binding is admissible ONLY when the requested policy permits its
1363	    # own expression kind; otherwise it is a discovery lead. Positive proof: membership in the policy's
1364	    # explicit allowlist, never absence.
1365	    if binding in NEVER_JOURNAL_EVIDENCE:
1366	        permitted = policy.permitted_expression_kinds
1367	        own_kinds = _BINDING_EXPRESSION_KINDS[binding]
1368	        if any(k in permitted for k in own_kinds):
1369	            if cls == C_CITATION:
1370	                return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'no content', 'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1371	            return ADMISSIBLE, {
1372	                'reason': f'{binding.lower().replace("_", " ")} admissible as its own expression under '
1373	                          f'the {policy.name} policy; {cinfo["reason"]}',
1374	                'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1375	        return DISCOVERY_LEAD, {
1376	            'reason': NEVER_JOURNAL_EVIDENCE[binding]
1377	                      + f'; the requested source policy ({policy.name}) does not permit its kind',
1378	            'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1379	    if binding == UNRESOLVED:
1380	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'we cannot show this text is the work we cite',
1381	                              'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1382	    if cls == C_CITATION:
1383	        return INADMISSIBLE, {'reason': 'no content', 'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1384	
1385	    # ---- THE POSTCONDITION. It does not care which branch answered, or in what order. -----------
1386	    assert binding not in NEVER_JOURNAL_EVIDENCE, (
1387	        f'ADMISSIBLE was about to be minted for a {binding} binding. THE V9 P0 IS REOPENED.')
1388	    return ADMISSIBLE, {'reason': f'{binding.lower().replace("_", " ")}; {cinfo["reason"]}',
1389	                        'content_class': cls, 'binding': binding}
1390	
1391

1393

1396	    """Sol: "high_quality" must render as its COMPONENTS, each with provenance.
1397	
1398	    There is deliberately NO `.label` and NO `__str__` that collapses to a scalar. A single word
1399	    ("high quality") is exactly the kind of claim that outruns its evidence — one strong component

440	    # WIDENING change to an accepted-manuscript detector: it can only move bytes OUT of journal_version,
441	    # never into it. Under a JOURNAL-ONLY contract that is the safe direction, and it is the true one.
442	    r'|author manuscript;? *available in pmc|\bnihms\s*\d{3,})', re.I)
443	# Typeset journal furniture: a running head with volume/issue/pages, or a publisher rights block.
444	_JOURNAL_MARK = re.compile(
445	    r'(volume \d+[,—-] *number \d+|vol\.? *\d+[,(]? *(no\.?|issue|\()? *\d+'
446	    r'|doi[:\s]*10\.\d{4,9}/|©\s*(the author|\d{4})|article reuse guidelines'
447	    r'|received .{0,40}(revised|accepted).{0,40}\d{4}'
448	    # ---- THE MASTHEAD FORMS THIS PATTERN COULD NOT SEE ------------------------------------------
449	    # Segmenting the cover sheet off (below) exposed a gap that the cover sheet had been HIDING: the
450	    # two commonest article-of-record mastheads in this corpus matched NOTHING here, and both real
451	    # journal articles were being rescued by the DOI THEIR COVER SHEET PRINTED. Remove the cover
452	    # sheet and the AER deposit fell to `unknown`. The furniture was always right there:
453	    #     "American Economic Review 2014, 104(8): 2509-2526"   <- vol(issue): page-range
454	    #     "AI and Ethics (2021) 1:119-130"                     <- (year) vol:page-range
455	    #     "https://doi.org/10.1007/..."                        <- `doi[:\s]*10\.` cannot match this:
456	    #                                                             ".org/" sits between `doi` and `10.`
457	    # Widening an ADMITTING mark is the dangerous direction, and it is safe ONLY because of where this
458	    # mark may now be read: the article's OWN front matter, never a cover sheet, and only after the
459	    # accepted-manuscript / working-paper / preprint marks have all declined to fire.
460	    r'|doi\.org/10\.\d{4,9}/'
461	    r'|\b\d{1,4} *\( *\d{1,3} *\) *: *\d{1,5} *[-–—] *\d{1,5}'
462	    r'|\(\d{4}\) *\d{1,4} *: *\d{1,5} *[-–—] *\d{1,5})', re.I)
