464	#: ══ A REPOSITORY COVER SHEET IS NOT THE DOCUMENT. THE *ONE* DEFINITION. ═════════════════════════
465	#:
466	#: THE SIXTH HOP OF THE V9 P0 LIVED IN EXACTLY THIS GAP. `_JOURNAL_MARK` above matches a bare DOI
467	#: string — and this file's own census docstring has warned, in writing, the whole time:
468	#:
469	#:     "`_JOURNAL_MARK` matches a DOI string — but a DOI string is printed by every repository COVER
470	#:      SHEET, which cites the article rather than being it."
471	#:
472	#: The warning was written and the code was never changed. `derive_expression_kind` read its marks out
473	#: of `text[:12000]` — cover sheet included — so a White Rose deposit of an ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT was
474	#: classified `journal_version` on the strength of the DOI ITS OWN COVER SHEET PRINTS, and
475	#: `resolve_attribution(JOURNAL_ONLY)` then ADMITTED it and would have printed "Acemoglu and Restrepo
476	#: (2020), Journal of Political Economy" over the manuscript's 0.37pp.
477	#:
478	#: THE RULE, AND IT IS NOT NEGOTIABLE:
479	#:   * the cover sheet MAY CONVICT — a library writing "this is an author produced version" is
480	#:     describing THE FILE IT DEPOSITED, and that is testimony about these bytes;
481	#:   * the cover sheet MAY NEVER ACQUIT — its DOI, its volume/issue, its citation block are the
482	#:     library CITING the article of record, which is precisely what a document that IS the article
483	#:     of record never needs to do.
484	#: Convicting evidence is read from the whole document; ACQUITTING evidence only from the article's
485	#: own front matter. (event_ledger imports this same pattern, so "what is a cover sheet" is defined
486	#: ONCE in this repo and cannot drift between the two lanes that ask.)
487	_COVER_SHEET = re.compile(
488	    r'(this is a repository copy of|this is an? (author|accepted|final)[- ]?(produced|version)'
489	    r'|white rose research online|enlighten|eprints|dspace|munin|hal (open science|id)'
490	    r'|citation for (the )?published version|the version (presented here|in the repository)'
491	    r'|downloaded from .{0,60}(repository|eprints|dspace|core\.ac\.uk)'
492	    r'|this version is available at|general rights|take down policy'
493	    r'|users may download and print one copy)', re.I)

496	def segment_cover_sheet(text: str, window: int = 12000) -> tuple[str, str]:
497	    """-> (cover_sheet_text, the_document_itself).
498	
499	    The boundary is the first form feed after the last cover-sheet marker — a cover sheet IS a
500	    separate PDF page, and that is the one structural thing reliably true of it. No marker => no cover
501	    sheet, and the document is the whole text (the common case, which must stay cheap).
502	    """
503	    head = text[:window]
504	    hits = list(_COVER_SHEET.finditer(head))
505	    if not hits:
506	        return '', text
507	    end = hits[-1].end()
508	    ff = text.find('\x0c', end)
509	    boundary = ff + 1 if 0 <= ff < window else end
510	    return text[:boundary], text[boundary:]

512	# =================================================================================================
513	# THE REGISTRY — the ONE table that says what each artifact kind IS and when it is COMPLETE.
514	# =================================================================================================
515	# Sol's point, made mechanical: completeness is a property OF A KIND, never of a word count.
516	#
517	# `stub_floor` separates THE DOCUMENT from A STUB OF IT, and it exists ONLY for the scholarly kinds,
518	# where a 500-word artifact is definitionally not the article (it is its abstract, or its landing
519	# page). For a judicial opinion, a statute section or a trial-registry record it is None: THOSE
520	# ARTIFACTS ARE COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. That is the whole difference between this and MIN_WORDS=2500,
521	# which asserted one number over every kind of document in the world.
522	#
523	# `expression` is the expression kind these bytes CONSTITUTE when they are the document — the link
524	# between "what is this artifact" and "what may a span in it name". A judicial opinion's bytes are its
525	# `official_text`; there is no journal version of it to widen to, ever.
526	#
527	# `needs_results` IS DELIBERATELY ABSENT. I first wrote it as a completeness gate and it declared six
528	# genuine journal articles incomplete — Technovation (15,985w), JEP (11,872w), AER (9,732w), JBR,
529	# Raj, Chalmers — because economics papers title their results sections after their CONTENT ("V. The
530	# Effect of Robots on Employment"), and a JEP essay has no results section at all. Gating on a
531	# section heading is the same error as gating on a word count, moved one axis over. The
532	# result-bearing sections are REPORTED in the profile (the miner weights by them) and GATE NOTHING.
533	
534	SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR = 1200     # scholarly kinds ONLY. It is not a fact about documents in general.
535	SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR = 120  # ...and below THIS, a scholarly fragment is a citation stub.

539	                                  expression='journal_version',     finding_bearing=True),

615	def judge_completeness(artifact_kind: str, n_words: int,
616	                       extraction_verdict: str = 'CLEAN') -> tuple[bool, list[str]]:
617	    """THE ONE COMPLETENESS REDUCER. `profile()`, `Graph.from_json()` and
618	    `event_ledger.derive_content_profile()` all come through here and through nothing else.
619	
620	    THERE IS NO UNIVERSAL WORD THRESHOLD IN THIS FUNCTION AND THERE MUST NEVER BE ONE. Completeness is
621	    read out of KIND_PROFILE for THIS kind: a judicial opinion, a statute section and a trial-registry
622	    record have stub_floor=None and are COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH. A cookie banner is incomplete at ANY
623	    length, because `landing_page` cannot bear a finding at all — which is the correct reason, and not
624	    a fact about its size.
625	
626	    The one length that is never enough is ZERO. "Complete at any length" is not complete at no length:
627	    a registry record we hold nothing of is not a short registry record.
628	    """
629	    spec = KIND_PROFILE.get(artifact_kind)
630	    if spec is None:
631	        raise ValueError(f'{artifact_kind!r} is not a registered artifact kind — an unregistered kind '
632	                         f'cannot be judged complete OR incomplete, and defaulting it to either is how '
633	                         f'a label outruns its evidence. Register it in KIND_PROFILE.')
634	    reasons: list[str] = []
635	    if not spec['finding_bearing']:
636	        reasons.append(f'artifact kind `{artifact_kind}` cannot carry a finding')
637	    if extraction_verdict == 'CORRUPT':
638	        reasons.append('bytes are not readable prose')
639	    if n_words <= 0:
640	        reasons.append('there are no bytes — complete at any length is not complete at NO length')
641	    elif spec['stub_floor'] is not None and n_words < spec['stub_floor']:
642	        reasons.append(f'{n_words} words is below the stub floor for `{artifact_kind}` '
643	                       f'({spec["stub_floor"]}) — this is a fragment of the document, not the document')
644	    return (not reasons), reasons

647	def _norm(s: str) -> str:
648	    return re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', (s or '')).strip()

650	
651	def extractability(text: str) -> dict:
652	    """Are these bytes even a readable rendering of a document? Derived, never asserted."""
653	    toks = _WORDY.findall(text.lower())
654	    n = len(toks) or 1
655	    sw = sum(1 for t in toks if t in _FUNCTION_WORDS) / n
656	    words = text.split() or ['']
657	    cid = len(_CID.findall(text)) / len(words)
658	    alpha = sum(c.isalpha() or c.isspace() for c in text) / max(1, len(text))
659	    if sw < CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY:
660	        verdict = 'CORRUPT'          # not English. A cipher, a glyph dump, or not prose at all.
661	    elif cid > DEGRADED_CID_RATIO:
662	        verdict = 'DEGRADED'         # readable body, but a non-embedded font ate part of it
663	    else:
664	        verdict = 'CLEAN'
665	    return dict(verdict=verdict, stopword_density=round(sw, 4),
666	                cid_ratio=round(cid, 4), alpha_ratio=round(alpha, 3))
667	
668	
669	# A body that is the paper CONTAINS THE PAPER'S OWN ABSTRACT'S VOCABULARY -- the abstract is a
670	# summary of it. Measured on this corpus: every genuine body scores 0.975-1.000 (7 of 9 at exactly
671	# 1.000). The one body that is a DIFFERENT PAPER scores 0.391. The threshold below sits in the middle
672	# of that empty gap; __main__ reprints the distribution so it can be re-derived rather than believed.
673	CONTRADICTED_VOCAB_OVERLAP = 0.70
674	_IDENT_MIN_ABSTRACT_WORDS = 40      # below this the abstract is too thin to be a probe
675	_IDENT_MIN_VOCAB = 10
676	_IDENT_MIN_BODY_WORDS = 1000        # below this the body is a stub; absence proves nothing
677	
678	
679	def identity_agreement(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
680	                       readable: bool = True) -> dict:
681	    """Do these bytes belong to the Work the row claims? THE CHECK THAT CATCHES A DIFFERENT PAPER.
682	
683	    Not one component asked this. A title matcher matched the phrase "Rise of the Machines" and
684	    handed us a MATHEMATICS PREPRINT; every downstream check then verified, correctly and
685	    irrelevantly, that the span was verbatim IN THE MATHEMATICS PREPRINT.
686	
687	    CONTRADICTED REQUIRES POSITIVE EVIDENCE OF A DIFFERENT WORK. It is never inferred from the
688	    ABSENCE of ours. My first version of this function inferred it from absence -- "none of the
689	    claimed authors appears in the text" -- and it convicted EIGHT papers, all innocent: seven were
690	    bare abstracts that simply do not carry an author block, and the eighth was Autor (2003), whose
691	    title page is (cid:N) font codes. Meanwhile the ONE genuinely foreign paper scored CONFIRMED,
692	    because its two-word title ("Rise of the Machines") appears verbatim in the maths paper.
693	    Absence of our evidence, read as evidence of absence, is the HTTP-429-means-"no free copy exists"
694	    error exactly. So the only route to CONTRADICTED is a positive, two-sided content test:
695	
696	        the row carries an abstract FROM THE BIBLIOGRAPHY (independent of whatever we fetched);
697	        the body we fetched does NOT contain that abstract's distinctive vocabulary;
698	        therefore the body is not a rendering of this work.
699	    """
700	    head = text[:20000]
701	    authors = [a for a in (work.authors or []) if len(a) > 2]
702	    named = [a for a in authors if re.search(r'\b' + re.escape(a) + r'\b', head, re.I)]
703	    twords = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (work.title or '').lower())}
704	    tfound = {w for w in twords if w in head.lower()}
705	    # A title of one or two distinctive words is not a test: "Rise of the Machines" yields {machines},
706	    # which the maths paper contains. Below three, the title signal is UNUSABLE, not passing.
707	    title_usable = len(twords) >= 3
708	
709	    overlap: float | None = None
710	    vocab = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{5,}', (independent_abstract or '').lower())
711	             if w not in _FUNCTION_WORDS}
712	    if (len((independent_abstract or '').split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_ABSTRACT_WORDS
713	            and len(vocab) >= _IDENT_MIN_VOCAB
714	            and len(text.split()) >= _IDENT_MIN_BODY_WORDS):
715	        low = text.lower()
716	        overlap = round(sum(1 for w in vocab if w in low) / len(vocab), 3)
717	
718	    if not readable:
719	        # You cannot judge identity from bytes you cannot read. Autor (2003)'s authors and title are
720	        # absent because they are (cid:11)(cid:3)... -- that is a fact about the FONT, not the paper.
721	        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'bytes are not readable — identity cannot be judged'
722	    elif overlap is not None and overlap < CONTRADICTED_VOCAB_OVERLAP:
723	        verdict = 'CONTRADICTED'
724	        basis = (f'the body contains only {overlap:.0%} of the distinctive vocabulary of this work’s '
725	                 f'own abstract (genuine bodies on this corpus: 97.5–100%) — THESE ARE NOT ITS BYTES')
726	    elif named:
727	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(named)}/{len(authors)} claimed authors named in the text'
728	    elif overlap is not None and overlap >= 0.90:
729	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'body contains {overlap:.0%} of its own abstract’s vocabulary'
730	    elif title_usable and len(tfound) >= 0.8 * len(twords):
731	        verdict, basis = 'CONFIRMED', f'{len(tfound)}/{len(twords)} distinctive title words present'
732	    else:
733	        verdict, basis = 'UNRESOLVED', 'no positive evidence either way (absence proves nothing)'
734	
735	    return dict(verdict=verdict, basis=basis,
736	                authors_named=len(named), authors_claimed=len(authors),
737	                title_words_found=len(tfound), title_words=len(twords), title_usable=title_usable,
738	                abstract_vocab_overlap=overlap)
739	
740	
741	def derive_expression_kind(text: str, work_kind: str = 'study') -> tuple[str, str]:
742	    """Which VERSION do these bytes say they are? Derived from self-identifying furniture.
743	
744	    Returns ('unknown', ...) freely. `unknown` is the honest answer for a PDF body with its title
745	    page mangled, and an honest `unknown` costs us evidence -- which is the correct price.
746	
747	    THE SCHOLARLY VERSION TAXONOMY APPLIES ONLY TO SCHOLARLY WORKS. There is no preprint of a statute
748	    and no accepted manuscript of a judicial opinion; the authoritative text IS the artifact, and the
749	    registry says so. Running the NBER/arXiv/"accepted manuscript" regexes over a court opinion does
750	    not find nothing — it finds whatever the opinion happens to QUOTE, which is how a whole-text scan
751	    for "NBER Working Paper" once read the bibliography and called the JEP article a working paper.
752	    """
753	    fam = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind or 'study')
754	    if fam is not None:
755	        ex = KIND_PROFILE[fam]['expression']
756	        return ex, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — the version taxonomy of a {fam.replace("_", " ")} is '
757	                    f'not the scholarly one; these bytes are its {ex.replace("_", " ")}')
758	    head = text[:12000]
759	    # THE COVER SHEET MAY CONVICT BUT MAY NEVER ACQUIT (see `_COVER_SHEET`). The three INADMISSIBLE
760	    # marks are read from the whole head — a library describing the file it deposited is testimony
761	    # about these bytes. The one ADMITTING mark is read ONLY from the article's own front matter,
762	    # because a cover sheet's DOI and citation block are the library CITING the article of record,
763	    # which is the one thing the article of record itself never has to do.
764	    cover, document = segment_cover_sheet(head)
765	    wp, pre, am = _WP_MARK.search(head), _PREPRINT_MARK.search(head), _AM_MARK.search(head)
766	    jr = _JOURNAL_MARK.search(document[:12000])
767	
768	    # Order still matters (an accepted manuscript carries the journal's citation block too), but it is
769	    # no longer LOAD-BEARING: the only branch that can return `journal_version` cannot see the cover
770	    # sheet at all, so no reordering of these four lines can promote a manuscript to the article of
771	    # record. The ordering is a courtesy to the ERROR MESSAGE, not the thing keeping the door shut.
772	    if am:
773	        return 'accepted_manuscript', f'bytes say: {am.group(0)[:48]!r}'
774	    if wp:
775	        return 'working_paper', f'bytes say: {wp.group(0)[:48]!r}'
776	    if pre:
777	        return 'preprint', f'bytes say: {pre.group(0)[:48]!r}'
778	    if jr:
779	        return 'journal_version', (f'typeset journal furniture in the ARTICLE\'S OWN front matter '
780	                                   f'(not on a cover sheet): {jr.group(0)[:48]!r}')
781	    if cover:
782	        return 'unknown', ('a repository cover sheet, and NO version furniture in the document under '
783	                           'it — the library\'s citation of the article is not the article')
784	    return 'unknown', 'no self-identifying version furniture in the first 12,000 chars'
785	
786	
787	def profile(text: str, work: Work, independent_abstract: str = '',
788	            fetch_outcome: str = 'ok') -> dict:
789	    """THE CONTENT-DERIVED ARTIFACT PROFILE. Replaces MIN_WORDS entirely.
790	
791	    Every field is derived FROM THE BYTES. `complete` is judged against the profile FOR THIS KIND,
792	    so a short judicial opinion is complete and a 535-word cookie banner is not.
793	    """
794	    text = text or ''
795	    words = len(text.split())
796	    ex = extractability(text)
797	    ident = identity_agreement(text, work, independent_abstract, readable=ex['verdict'] == 'CLEAN')
798	
799	    view = View(text)
800	    secs = sections_of(view)
801	    present = sorted({name for _, _, name in secs if name not in ('front', 'body')})
802	    result_secs = [s for s in present if s in RESULT_BEARING]   # REPORTED. GATES NOTHING. See above.
803	    # body/chrome ratio, measured on the bytes -- not on a word count.
804	    chrome_hits = len(_CHROME.findall(text))
805	    body_chars = sum(e - s for s, e, name in secs if SECTION_WEIGHT.get(name, 0.5) > 0)
806	    body_ratio = round(body_chars / max(1, len(text)), 3)
807	    chrome_per_kw = round(1000 * chrome_hits / max(1, words), 2)
808	
809	    work_kind = work.kind or 'study'
810	    family = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind)      # None => scholarly; else the kind is FIXED by the work
811	    ekind, ebasis = derive_expression_kind(text, work_kind=work_kind)
812	
813	    # ---- artifact_kind: DERIVED, in strict order of what the bytes can actually PROVE ------------
814	    # THE BYTES MAY ALWAYS DEMOTE. They may never promote. A judicial opinion whose fetch returned a
815	    # login wall is a landing_page, not a short opinion — so the demotions run FIRST, above the work's
816	    # own kind. But nothing in a stranger's letterhead can turn a statute into a working paper, so the
817	    # work's kind runs ABOVE the scholarly version furniture.
818	    if fetch_outcome != 'ok':
819	        # A failed fetch is a fact about our REQUEST, never a fact about the world.
820	        kind, basis = 'unknown', f'fetch outcome: {fetch_outcome}'
821	    elif ident['verdict'] == 'CONTRADICTED':
822	        # The single most important branch in this file. These bytes are SOMEONE ELSE'S PAPER.
823	        kind, basis = 'wrong_work', ident['basis']
824	    elif ex['verdict'] == 'CORRUPT':
825	        kind, basis = 'extraction_failure', (
826	            f"stopword density {ex['stopword_density']} < {CORRUPT_STOPWORD_DENSITY} — "
827	            f"these bytes are not English prose")
828	    elif chrome_hits >= 2 and (chrome_per_kw >= 5 or (family is None and words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR)):
829	        # WEB FURNITURE WITH NO DOCUMENT UNDER IT. Note that the AEA landing page for Autor (2015)
830	        # carries the article's own "vol. 29, no. 3" citation block -- so journal furniture proves
831	        # only that a page CITES the article, never that it IS the article. This test runs first for
832	        # exactly that reason.
833	        # The LENGTH half of this test is scholarly-only: a 300-word statute section is a statute
834	        # section, and only the chrome DENSITY may call it a web page.
835	        kind, basis = 'landing_page', (
836	            f'{chrome_hits} web-chrome markers ({chrome_per_kw}/1k words) over {words} words — '
837	            f'this is a web page about the document, not the document')
838	    elif family is not None:
839	        # A CASE, A STATUTE, A TRIAL RECORD. Its completeness is not a word count and never was.
840	        kind, basis = family, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — this is its {family.replace("_", " ")}, '
841	                               f'which is COMPLETE AT ANY LENGTH (registry stub_floor=None)')
842	    elif ekind == 'journal_version':
843	        kind, basis = 'journal_article', ebasis
844	    elif ekind == 'proceedings_version':
845	        kind, basis = 'proceedings_paper', ebasis
846	    elif ekind in ('working_paper', 'preprint', 'accepted_manuscript'):
847	        kind, basis = ekind, ebasis
848	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_ABSTRACT_FLOOR:
849	        kind, basis = 'citation_only', f'{words} words — a citation stub, not even an abstract'
850	    elif words < SCHOLARLY_STUB_FLOOR:
851	        kind, basis = 'abstract', f'{words} words with no version furniture — a stub, not the article'
852	    else:
853	        kind, basis = 'unknown', ebasis
854	
855	    # ---- completeness: THE ONE REDUCER, driven by the registry FOR THIS KIND ---------------------
856	    complete, reasons = judge_completeness(kind, words, ex['verdict'])
857	
858	    return dict(
859	        artifact_kind=kind, artifact_kind_basis=basis,
860	        expression_kind=ekind, expression_kind_basis=ebasis,
861	        complete=complete, incomplete_because=reasons,
862	        n_words=words, sections_present=present, result_bearing_sections=result_secs,
863	        body_ratio=body_ratio, chrome_markers=chrome_hits, chrome_per_1k_words=chrome_per_kw,
864	        extractability=ex, identity=ident, fetch_outcome=fetch_outcome,
865	    )
866	
867	
868	# =================================================================================================
869	# 3. THE GRAPH
870	# =================================================================================================
871	
872	class SpanBindingError(ValueError):
873	    """A span that cannot be bound to real bytes. Not a warning: THE BINDING DOES NOT EXIST."""
874	
875	
876	class GraphIntegrityError(ValueError):
877	    """A graph on disk that does not agree with itself. It is REFUSED, never repaired."""
878	
879

1295	    def resolve_attribution(self, ref: dict | str,
1296	                            source_policy: SourcePolicy = ANY_VERSION) -> Attribution:
1297	        """MAY THIS BOUND SPAN be attributed under THIS task's instruction, and TO WHAT ID?
1298	
1299	        Sol V9 §4 moved this API from manifestation-wide permission to BINDING-SPECIFIC permission:
1300	
1301	            resolve_attribution(binding, attribution_policy)
1302	
1303	        `ref` is therefore a BINDING (the dict `bind_span()` returns, which is also what a card carries).
1304	        A bare `manifestation_id` string is still accepted — the honest whole-document question, asked by
1305	        the census and by the quarantine sweep — and it answers WITHOUT any per-span widening: a
1306	        manifestation-wide question can only be answered with manifestation-wide evidence, i.e. the
1307	        document's own expression plus a whole-document `exact_copy_of`. A SpanCorrespondence grants ONE
1308	        SPAN and therefore cannot answer a question that was not asked about a span.
1309	
1310	        Two independent conditions, BOTH required:
1311	          1. the bytes are usable as evidence at all — complete, uncorrupted, the right work; AND
1312	          2. some expression this span MAY LEGALLY NAME is of a kind THIS POLICY PERMITS.
1313	
1314	        A span from a corrupt PDF of the real journal article is still inadmissible -- see Weiss, whose
1315	        verbatim span said "633 per cent" because the PDF was a Caesar-shifted encoding of "300".
1316	        """
1317	        if isinstance(ref, str):
1318	            manifestation_id, bid, targets_of = ref, None, self.attribution_targets
1319	            binding = None
1320	        else:
1321	            binding = ref
1322	            manifestation_id = binding.get('manifestation_id')
1323	            bid = binding.get('binding_id')
1324	            targets_of = None
1325	
1326	        m = self.manifestations.get(manifestation_id)
1327	        if m is None:
1328	            raise KeyError(f'no manifestation {manifestation_id!r}')
1329	
1330	        if binding is None:
1331	            targets = tuple(targets_of(manifestation_id))
1332	        else:
1333	            # THE BINDING IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT. It has been through a JSON file, and a binding whose
1334	            # offsets nobody rechecked is `text[900:100]` — empty, self-consistent, and citing nothing.
1335	            if not self.verify_span(binding):
1336	                return Attribution(
1337	                    manifestation_id=manifestation_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
1338	                    expression_id=m.expression_id, permitted_expression_ids=(),
1339	                    policy=source_policy.name, admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
1340	                    binding_id=bid,
1341	                    refusal=('this binding does not verify against the bytes it names — the offsets, the '
1342	                             'hash, the stored span or the permitted set disagree with the manifestation'),
1343	                    identity_verdict=m.profile.get('semantic_binding'),
1344	                    disposition=DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE, reason_code=RC_SPAN_BINDING_INVALID)
1345	            targets = tuple(self.attribution_targets_for_binding(binding))
1346	
1347	        base = dict(manifestation_id=manifestation_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
1348	                    expression_id=m.expression_id, permitted_expression_ids=targets,
1349	                    policy=source_policy.name, binding_id=bid)
1350	
1351	        # ── IDENTITY GATE (Sol binding-gate §1) — PRECEDES completeness and expression policy ────────
1352	        # A span may be ATTRIBUTED only when these bytes are POSITIVELY PROVEN to be the requested Work.
1353	        # This is the universal naming boundary: census, quarantine sweep, miner preflight, card
1354	        # construction, publisher, and report-AST all converge here, so no caller can acquire attribution
1355	        # by bypassing miner selection. ALLOWLIST — a missing/unknown/stale verdict rejects by default.
1356	        # Import ONLY the canonical allowlist and the two explicit negative verdicts (Sol §1, edit 4).
1357	        # The version constants are NOT consulted here — identity is orthogonal to which expression a
1358	        # proven work may name, and an unused import is a place a future edit reaches for the wrong tool.
1359	        from event_ledger import (IDENTITY_PROVEN as _IDOK, DIFFERENT_WORK as _DW,  # noqa: PLC0415
1360	                                   UNRESOLVED as _UNR)
1361	        _iv = m.profile.get('semantic_binding')
1362	        if _iv not in _IDOK:
1363	            if _iv == _DW:
1364	                _disp, _rc = DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE, RC_IDENTITY_DIFFERENT_WORK
1365	                _why = ('these bytes are a DIFFERENT WORK than the one this span would cite (positive '
1366	                        'evidence of a stranger — foreign front-matter DOI or disjoint byline); '
1367	                        'attributing them to this source would be fabrication')
1368	            elif _iv == _UNR:
1369	                _disp, _rc = DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY, RC_IDENTITY_UNRESOLVED
1370	                _why = ('identity is NOT positively proven for these bytes (UNRESOLVED_BINDING) — a lead '
1371	                        'for retrieval/coverage, but it may not be attributed to this source')
1372	            else:
1373	                _disp, _rc = DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE, RC_IDENTITY_UNKNOWN_VERDICT
1374	                _why = (f'no positive identity verdict is on these bytes ({_iv!r} is not in the proven '
1375	                        f'allowlist) — fail closed, name nothing')
1376	            return Attribution(
1377	                manifestation_id=manifestation_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
1378	                expression_id=m.expression_id, permitted_expression_ids=(),
1379	                policy=source_policy.name, binding_id=bid,
1380	                admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None, refusal=_why,
1381	                identity_verdict=_iv, disposition=_disp, reason_code=_rc)
1382	
1383	        # NOTE: a DERIVATION_CONFLICT gate (identity says preprint/accepted, expression node claims a
1384	        # more-published version — the medRxiv/working-paper-as-journal P0) belongs HERE. Its MECHANISM
1385	        # is a Sol design decision, pending. Do not invent it inline.
1386	
1387	        if source_policy.require_complete and not m.profile.get('complete'):
1388	            why = '; '.join(m.profile.get('incomplete_because') or ['the bytes are not a usable document'])
1389	            # An integrity failure: permitted set is emptied — nothing may be named from unusable bytes.
1390	            return Attribution(
1391	                manifestation_id=manifestation_id, content_hash=m.content_hash,
1392	                expression_id=m.expression_id, permitted_expression_ids=(),
1393	                policy=source_policy.name, binding_id=bid,
1394	                admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
1395	                refusal=f'these bytes may not carry a finding: {why}',
1396	                identity_verdict=_iv, disposition=DISPOSITION_QUARANTINE,
1397	                reason_code=RC_INCOMPLETE_BYTES)
1398	
1399	        # Prefer the expression whose bytes these ACTUALLY ARE. Widening is a fallback, never a first
1400	        # choice: naming the document we hold is always the strongest true thing we can say.
1401	        permitted = [t for t in targets
1402	                     if self.expressions[t].kind in source_policy.permitted_expression_kinds]
1403	        if not permitted:
1404	            kinds = ', '.join(sorted({self.expressions[t].kind for t in targets})) or 'nothing'
1405	            return Attribution(
1406	                **base, admitted=False, names_expression_id=None, text=None,
1407	                refusal=(f'policy `{source_policy.name}` permits '
1408	                         f'{{{", ".join(source_policy.permitted_expression_kinds)}}}, and a span here '
1409	                         f'may only name {{{kinds}}}. No ASSERTED span-preserving edge widens it — '
1410	                         f'citing a permitted source with THESE bytes would be fabrication'),
1411	                identity_verdict=_iv, disposition=DISPOSITION_LEAD_ONLY,
1412	                reason_code=RC_VERSION_NOT_PERMITTED)
1413	        chosen = m.expression_id if m.expression_id in permitted else permitted[0]
1414	        return Attribution(**base, admitted=True, names_expression_id=chosen,
1415	                           text=self.expressions[chosen].attribution, refusal=None,
1416	                           identity_verdict=_iv, disposition=DISPOSITION_ADMIT, reason_code=RC_ADMITTED)
1417	
1418	    def verify_span(self, binding: dict) -> bool:
1419	        """The enforcement point. A binding arriving here is AN UNTRUSTED INPUT — by now it has been
1420	        through a JSON file — so this re-checks EVERYTHING bind_span() checked, and then the bytes.
1421	
1422	        A gate that trusts the offsets it is handed is a gate that verifies `text[900:100] == ''`.
1423	        """
1424	        try:
1425	            mid = binding['manifestation_id']
1426	            s, e = binding['span_start'], binding['span_end']
1427	            stored, chash = binding['text'], binding['content_hash']
1428	        except (KeyError, TypeError):
1429	            return False
1430	        m = self.manifestations.get(mid)
1431	        if m is None or m.content_hash != chash:
1432	            return False
1433	        for v in (s, e):
1434	            if isinstance(v, bool) or not isinstance(v, int):
1435	                return False
1436	        if s < 0 or e <= s or e > len(m.text):
1437	            return False
1438	        # the node itself must not have been edited since it was loaded: the hash is over THE TEXT.
1439	        if hashlib.sha256(m.text.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest() != m.content_hash:
1440	            return False
1441	        if m.text[s:e] != stored:
1442	            return False
1443	        # ...and the permitted set is not a cache. If an edge changed, or a SpanCorrespondence stopped
1444	        # verifying, the STORED permission is stale — and a stale permission is how a span keeps naming
1445	        # a journal it was never allowed to name. Recomputed PER SPAN, because that is now the unit at
1446	        # which permission exists.
1447	        if 'permitted_expression_ids' in binding:
1448	            live = self.attribution_targets_for_binding(
1449	                dict(manifestation_id=mid, span_start=s, span_end=e))
1450	            if list(binding['permitted_expression_ids']) != live:
1451	                return False
1452	        return True
1453	
1454	    # -- derived conveniences (they go THROUGH the API above; they are not a second door) ----------
1455	    def journal_attributable(self, manifestation_id: str) -> bool:
1456	        """May a span from these bytes be printed as a finding OF A JOURNAL ARTICLE? (task 72)"""
1457	        return self.resolve_attribution(manifestation_id, JOURNAL_ONLY).admitted
1458	
1459	    # -- serialisation ----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1460	    def to_json(self) -> dict:
1461	        return dict(
1462	            works=[asdict(w) for w in self.works.values()],
1463	            expressions=[asdict(e) for e in self.expressions.values()],
1464	            # text is retained on disk too: NOTHING IS EVER DELETED.
1465	            manifestations=[asdict(m) for m in self.manifestations.values()],
1466	            edges=[asdict(e) for e in self.edges],
1467	            correspondences=[asdict(c) for c in self.correspondences],
1468	        )
1469	
1470	    @classmethod
1471	    def from_json(cls, data: dict | str) -> 'Graph':
1472	        """STRICT LOAD. A GRAPH ON DISK IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT.
1473	
1474	        Every check here exists because the file it reads is the same KIND of artifact that lied to us

1471	    def from_json(cls, data: dict | str) -> 'Graph':
1472	        """STRICT LOAD. A GRAPH ON DISK IS AN UNTRUSTED INPUT.
1473	
1474	        Every check here exists because the file it reads is the same KIND of artifact that lied to us
1475	        already. `to_json()` writes the text, its hash, its word count and its derived profile, and any
1476	        copy of that file — hand-edited, half-written, merged, or produced by an older version of this
1477	        module — can disagree with itself in every one of those places. A loader that accepts what it
1478	        is handed re-opens the hole FROM THE OTHER SIDE: the enforcement point would read a
1479	        `complete: true` that no bytes support, and would enforce it.
1480	
1481	        REFUSES, NEVER REPAIRS:
1482	          - a content_hash that is not sha256(text)                  [the span gate's only anchor]
1483	          - an n_words that is not len(text.split())                 [the count that describes a
1484	                                                                       document we no longer hold —
1485	                                                                       the exact defect event_ledger
1486	                                                                       catches in the live corpus]
1487	          - a stored extractability verdict the text does not support
1488	          - a `complete: true` (or false) THE REGISTRY DOES NOT DERIVE for that kind and that text
1489	          - a manifestation naming an expression or a work that does not exist
1490	          - a manifestation whose expression belongs to a DIFFERENT work
1491	          - an expression naming a work that does not exist
1492	          - an edge whose endpoints do not exist, or that carries no basis
1493	          - an ASSERTED span-preserving edge whose basis does not AUTHENTICATE span preservation
1494	          - any unknown field, anywhere (a schema drift silently drops the field a gate reads)
1495	        """
1496	        if isinstance(data, str):
1497	            data = json.loads(data)
1498	        g = cls()
1499	        err: list[str] = []
1500	
1501	        def _fields(cl) -> set[str]:
1502	            return set(cl.__dataclass_fields__)
1503	
1504	        def _required(cl) -> set[str]:
1505	            # A field is REQUIRED iff it declares neither a default nor a default_factory. This is read
1506	            # MECHANICALLY from the dataclass, so a new field with a default never becomes a phantom
1507	            # "missing" error and a new field WITHOUT a default is enforced automatically — no handwritten
1508	            # exemption list (the old `{'profile', 'kind'}`) to drift out of sync with the schema.
1509	            return {f.name for f in dataclasses.fields(cl)
1510	                    if f.default is dataclasses.MISSING
1511	                    and f.default_factory is dataclasses.MISSING}  # type: ignore[misc]
1512	
1513	        def _take(cl, d: dict, what: str):
1514	            extra = set(d) - _fields(cl)
1515	            if extra:
1516	                err.append(f'{what}: unknown field(s) {sorted(extra)} — this graph was not written by '
1517	                           f'this schema, and a field a gate reads may be missing from it')
1518	                return None
1519	            missing = _required(cl) - set(d)
1520	            if missing:
1521	                err.append(f'{what}: missing field(s) {sorted(missing)}')
1522	                return None
1523	            return cl(**d)
1524	
1525	        for d in data.get('works', []):
1526	            w = _take(Work, d, f"work {d.get('id')!r}")
1527	            if w is None:
1528	                continue
1529	            if w.kind not in WORK_KINDS:
1530	                err.append(f'work {w.id!r}: kind {w.kind!r} is not a registered work kind {WORK_KINDS}')
1531	            g.works[w.id] = w
1532	
1533	        for d in data.get('expressions', []):
1534	            e = _take(Expression, d, f"expression {d.get('id')!r}")
1535	            if e is None:
1536	                continue
1537	            if e.kind not in EXPRESSION_KINDS:
1538	                err.append(f'expression {e.id!r}: kind {e.kind!r} is not in EXPRESSION_KINDS')
1539	            if e.work_id not in g.works:
1540	                err.append(f'expression {e.id!r}: names work {e.work_id!r}, WHICH DOES NOT EXIST')
1541	            g.expressions[e.id] = e
1542	
1543	        for d in data.get('manifestations', []):
1544	            m = _take(Manifestation, d, f"manifestation {d.get('id')!r}")
1545	            if m is None:
1546	                continue
1547	            g.manifestations[m.id] = m
1548	            where = f'manifestation {m.id!r}'
1549	
1550	            # 1. THE HASH IS THE ONLY ANCHOR THE SPAN GATE HAS.
1551	            real = hashlib.sha256((m.text or '').encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest()
1552	            if real != m.content_hash:
1553	                err.append(f'{where}: content_hash {m.content_hash[:12]}… IS NOT sha256(text) '
1554	                           f'({real[:12]}…) — every span bound to it is bound to nothing')
1555	                continue
1556	
1557	            # 2. references resolve, and resolve TO THE SAME WORK.
1558	            if m.expression_id not in g.expressions:
1559	                err.append(f'{where}: names expression {m.expression_id!r}, WHICH DOES NOT EXIST')
1560	            elif g.expressions[m.expression_id].work_id != m.work_id:
1561	                err.append(f'{where}: is filed under work {m.work_id!r} but its expression '
1562	                           f'{m.expression_id!r} belongs to work '
1563	                           f'{g.expressions[m.expression_id].work_id!r}')
1564	            if m.work_id not in g.works:
1565	                err.append(f'{where}: names work {m.work_id!r}, WHICH DOES NOT EXIST')
1566	
1567	            # 3. the counts and labels stored beside the text must be THE ONES THE TEXT EARNS.
1568	            words = len((m.text or '').split())
1569	            if m.n_words != words:
1570	                err.append(f'{where}: n_words={m.n_words:,} but the text holds {words:,} — the count '
1571	                           f'describes a document this node no longer carries')
1572	            prof = m.profile or {}
1573	            kind = prof.get('artifact_kind')
1574	            if kind not in KIND_PROFILE:
1575	                err.append(f'{where}: artifact_kind {kind!r} is not a registered kind')
1576	                continue
1577	            verdict = extractability(m.text or '')['verdict']
1578	            stored_v = (prof.get('extractability') or {}).get('verdict')
1579	            if stored_v != verdict:
1580	                err.append(f'{where}: stored extractability {stored_v!r} but the bytes are {verdict!r}')
1581	            # THE ONE REDUCER, run again, against the bytes on disk. A `complete` flag nobody can
1582	            # re-derive is a label that asserts more than its content supports — with nothing checking.
1583	            ok, reasons = judge_completeness(kind, words, verdict)
1584	            if bool(prof.get('complete')) != ok:
1585	                err.append(f'{where}: profile says complete={prof.get("complete")!r}, but the registry '
1586	                           f'derives complete={ok!r} for a `{kind}` of {words:,} words '
1587	                           f'({"; ".join(reasons) if reasons else "no objection"})')
1588	
1589	            # 4. THE IDENTITY VERDICT, re-derived from the bytes through the ONE reducer (Sol §1, inv 5).
1590	            # A stored `semantic_binding` the bytes do not earn is the graph-tampering attack: edit
1591	            # DIFFERENT_WORK -> SAME_WORK on disk and the naming boundary would honour it. We never USE
1592	            # the stored value — we RE-DERIVE both verdict AND basis, and refuse any disagreement, exactly
1593	            # as for hash/completeness. This is the STRICT graph path (Sol §5): a missing or unknown stored
1594	            # verdict is an INTEGRITY ERROR here, not an old-file compatibility shim. `migrate()` is the
1595	            # only compatibility path for legacy corpus rows; strict load must never silently migrate one.
1596	            _wk = g.works.get(m.work_id)
1597	            if _wk is not None:
1598	                from event_ledger import (observe_text as _obs,  # noqa: PLC0415
1599	                                          derive_binding_core as _bind2,
1600	                                          SAME_WORK as _SW, VERSION_PUBLISHED as _VPUB,
1601	                                          VERSION_ACCEPTED as _VACC, VERSION_PREPRINT as _VPRE,
1602	                                          DIFFERENT_WORK as _DW, UNRESOLVED as _UNR)
1603	                _known_verdicts = {_SW, _VPUB, _VACC, _VPRE, _DW, _UNR}
1604	                _live, _live_ev = _bind2(requested_doi=_wk.doi or '', requested_title=_wk.title or '',
1605	                                         requested_authors=list(_wk.authors or []), prof=_obs(m.text or ''))
1606	                _stored_sb = prof.get('semantic_binding')
1607	                if _stored_sb is None:
1608	                    err.append(f'{where}: no stored semantic_binding — a strict graph must carry a '
1609	                               f're-derivable identity verdict; this file is not one this schema wrote')
1610	                elif _stored_sb not in _known_verdicts:
1611	                    err.append(f'{where}: stored semantic_binding {_stored_sb!r} is not a known verdict '
1612	                               f'{sorted(_known_verdicts)} — fail closed on an unknown identity token')
1613	                elif _stored_sb != _live:
1614	                    err.append(f'{where}: stored semantic_binding {_stored_sb!r} but the bytes re-derive '
1615	                               f'{_live!r} — identity may not be promoted by editing the file')
1616	                # USE the byte-derived verdict AND basis, never the stored ones. A stored row-cache verdict
1617	                # is never consulted here (see `row_binding_cache`, which is audit-only).
1618	                prof['semantic_binding'] = _live
1619	                prof['semantic_binding_basis'] = _live_ev
1620	
1621	        for i, d in enumerate(data.get('edges', [])):
1622	            e = _take(Edge, d, f'edge #{i}')
1623	            if e is None:
1624	                continue
1625	            g.edges.append(e)
1626	            where = f'edge #{i} {e.src!r} -{e.type}-> {e.dst!r}'
1627	            if e.type not in EDGE_TYPES:
1628	                err.append(f'{where}: unknown edge type {e.type!r}')
1629	            if e.status not in ('PROPOSED', 'ASSERTED'):
1630	                err.append(f'{where}: status must be PROPOSED or ASSERTED, not {e.status!r}')
1631	            for end in (e.src, e.dst):
1632	                if end not in g.expressions:
1633	                    err.append(f'{where}: endpoint {end!r} DOES NOT EXIST')
1634	            if not (e.basis or '').strip():
1635	                err.append(f'{where}: carries NO BASIS')
1636	            if e.status == 'ASSERTED' and e.type in SPAN_PRESERVING:
1637	                why = authentication_failure(e.basis) or g.exact_copy_failure(e.src, e.dst)
1638	                if why:
1639	                    err.append(f'{where}: ASSERTED span-preserving edge — {why}')
1640	
1641	        # ---- CORRESPONDENCES. Every one of them is RE-VERIFIED AGAINST THE BYTES ON DISK. ---------
1642	        # A correspondence is the ONLY per-span door out of a document, so a file that could smuggle one
1643	        # in would be a file that could make any manuscript's number into any journal's finding. The
1644	        # loader runs the SAME verifier the resolver runs — hashes, offsets, exact canonical equality —
1645	        # and refuses the whole graph if any of them fails. An edge that cannot be constructed but CAN
1646	        # BE LOADED is not a rule; it is a speed bump, and we have been over that speed bump already.
1647	        for i, d in enumerate(data.get('correspondences', [])):
1648	            sc = _take(SpanCorrespondence, d, f'correspondence #{i}')
1649	            if sc is None:
1650	                continue
1651	            g.correspondences.append(sc)
1652	            ok, why = g.verify_correspondence(sc)
1653	            if not ok:
1654	                err.append(f'correspondence #{i} {sc.id!r} '
1655	                           f'({sc.source_manifestation_id} -> {sc.target_manifestation_id}) '
1656	                           f'DOES NOT VERIFY: ' + '; '.join(why))
1657	
1658	        if err:
1659	            raise GraphIntegrityError(
1660	                f'{len(err)} integrity failure(s); THIS GRAPH IS NOT LOADED:\n  - ' + '\n  - '.join(err))

1668	def _slug(s: str, n: int = 40) -> str:
1669	    return re.sub(r'[^a-z0-9]+', '-', (s or '').lower()).strip('-')[:n] or 'x'
1670

1672	def derive_source_type(row: dict) -> tuple[str, str, str]:
1673	    """WHAT KIND OF SOURCE DOES THIS ROW DECLARE ITSELF TO BE?
1674	    -> (work_kind, the expression the metadata CLAIMS exists, basis)
1675	
1676	    `migrate()` used to open with `jid = f'{wid}:journal_version'` for every row it was ever handed.
1677	    Sol: "That is not valid for a judicial opinion or statute." It is not even valid for a PREPRINT
1678	    row — a posted-content record claims no journal version at all, and minting one creates, out of
1679	    nothing, a node whose whole meaning is "a peer-reviewed article exists here", for a work that may
1680	    never have been through peer review.
1681	
1682	    An empty claimed-expression is a LEGAL ANSWER and migrate() then mints no node. A row that names
1683	    no venue claims no published version, and the honest record of that is an absence — not a
1684	    `journal_version` whose attribution string renders as "Smith (2020), None".
1685	    """
1686	    declared = _norm(str(row.get('type') or '')).lower()
1687	    if declared in SOURCE_TYPE:
1688	        wk, ex = SOURCE_TYPE[declared]
1689	        if ex in ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version') and not _norm(row.get('venue') or ''):
1690	            return wk, '', (f'row declares type={declared!r} but names NO venue — there is no journal '
1691	                            f'to name, so no journal expression is claimed')
1692	        return wk, ex, f'row declares type={declared!r}'
1693	    if row.get('nct_id') or re.match(r'\s*(NCT\d{8}|ISRCTN\d+|EUCTR)', str(row.get('id') or ''), re.I):
1694	        return 'trial', 'registry_record', 'the row carries a trial-registry identifier'
1695	    if row.get('court') or row.get('case_name'):
1696	        return 'case', 'official_text', 'the row carries a court/case field'
1697	    if row.get('statute') or row.get('section'):
1698	        return 'statute', 'official_text', 'the row carries a statute/section field'
1699	    if row.get('doi') and _norm(row.get('venue') or ''):
1700	        return 'study', 'journal_version', 'no declared type; the row carries a DOI and a venue'
1701	    return 'study', '', (f'the row declares type={declared or None!r} and carries no venue — it claims '
1702	                         f'NO published expression, so none is minted')
1703

1705	def _attribution_for(kind: str, work: Work) -> str:
1706	    """The sentence a writer may RENDER for this expression. DISPLAY ONLY.
1707	
1708	    Whether it is legal in THIS answer is NOT decided here and is not stored anywhere — it is derived
1709	    per policy by `Graph.resolve_attribution()`. Sol: "If only the working paper is available, citing
1710	    it VIOLATES THE JOURNAL-ONLY INSTRUCTION, so it stays OUTSIDE THE ANSWER BODY." That is the
1711	    JOURNAL_ONLY policy refusing it, not a boolean baked into the node.
1712	    """
1713	    who = ' and '.join(work.authors[:2]) + (' et al.' if len(work.authors) > 2 else '')
1714	    yr = work.year
1715	    venue = work.venue or ''
1716	    if kind in ('official_text', 'registry_record'):
1717	        # A case or a statute is named by ITS OWN TITLE and its court/reporter/registry — not by
1718	        # "authors (year)". `Smith v. Jones (1998), Court of Appeal`.
1719	        return f'{work.title} ({yr}), {venue}' if venue else f'{work.title} ({yr})'
1720	    if not venue:
1721	        return f'{who} ({yr}), UNNAMED VENUE [may not be cited]'
1722	    if kind in ('journal_version', 'proceedings_version'):
1723	        return f'{who} ({yr}), {venue}'
1724	    if kind == 'accepted_manuscript':
1725	        # Legal ONLY if authenticated as the AM *of the journal version* -- that edge, not this label.
1726	        return f'{who} ({yr}), accepted manuscript of {venue}'
1727	    if kind == 'working_paper':
1728	        return f'{who} ({yr}), working paper [NOT the {venue} article]'
1729	    if kind == 'preprint':
1730	        return f'{who} ({yr}), preprint [NOT the {venue} article]'
1731	    return f'{who} ({yr}), UNIDENTIFIED VERSION [may not be cited]'
1732

1734	def ensure_work(g: Graph, *, doi: str, title: str, authors: list[str], year, venue: str,
1735	                source_type: str = '') -> tuple[Work, str, str]:
1736	    """The Work node, and the expression its METADATA CLAIMS EXISTS. -> (work, claimed_id, claimed_kind)
1737	
1738	    The claimed expression is real but WE MAY NOT HOLD ITS BYTES: it gets a node with no manifestation,
1739	    and that emptiness is the honest record of what we do not have.
1740	    """
1741	    wid = 'work:' + (_slug(doi) if doi else _slug(title))
1742	    row = dict(doi=doi, title=title, venue=venue, type=source_type)
1743	    work_kind, claimed_kind, claimed_basis = derive_source_type(row)
1744	    work = Work(id=wid, title=title or '', authors=list(authors or []), year=year,
1745	                venue=_norm(venue or '').replace('&', '&'), doi=doi or None, kind=work_kind)
1746	    g.works.setdefault(wid, work)
1747	    work = g.works[wid]
1748	
1749	    claimed_id = ''
1750	    if claimed_kind:
1751	        claimed_id = f'{wid}:{claimed_kind}'
1752	        g.expressions.setdefault(claimed_id, Expression(
1753	            id=claimed_id, work_id=wid, kind=claimed_kind,
1754	            kind_basis=f'claimed_by_metadata ({claimed_basis}; bytes NOT verified)',
1755	            attribution=_attribution_for(claimed_kind, work)))
1756	    return work, claimed_id, claimed_kind

1765	    through here, and they must: two functions that each decide what an expression is would be two
1766	    answers to "may this span name the journal", and the one that ships is whichever ran last.
1767	    """
1768	    wid = work.id
1769	    prof = profile(text, work, independent_abstract=independent_abstract)
1770	    ekind = prof['expression_kind']

1759	def ingest_bytes(g: Graph, work: Work, text: str, *, text_field: str, fetched_by: str,
1760	                 locator: str | None, locator_status: str, claimed_id: str, claimed_kind: str,
1761	                 independent_abstract: str = '') -> str:
1762	    """THE ONE PLACE BYTES BECOME A TYPED NODE. -> manifestation id.
1763	
1764	    `migrate()` (corpus rows) and `provenance_construct.construct()` (the event ledger) BOTH come
1765	    through here, and they must: two functions that each decide what an expression is would be two
1766	    answers to "may this span name the journal", and the one that ships is whichever ran last.
1767	    """
1768	    wid = work.id
1769	    prof = profile(text, work, independent_abstract=independent_abstract)
1770	    ekind = prof['expression_kind']
1771	    ebasis = prof['expression_kind_basis']
1772	
1773	    # ── IDENTITY, BY THE ONE SEMANTIC RULE (Sol binding-gate §1, invariants 1-3) ─────────────────
1774	    # The graph must re-derive identity FROM THE BYTES through the SAME reducer the ledger uses — never
1775	    # a provenance-specific approximation, and never the row's cached verdict. A DIFFERENT_WORK verdict
1776	    # (positive foreign DOI or disjoint byline) is a STRANGER'S PAPER: it takes the quarantine path
1777	    # below, exactly like an artifact that is not a rendering of this work. UNRESOLVED stays retained
1778	    # and attributable to nobody (resolve_attribution enforces lead-only). Deferred import: event_ledger
1779	    # imports provenance at module top, so this must be lazy to avoid a cycle.
1780	    from event_ledger import observe_text as _observe, derive_binding_core as _bind  # noqa: PLC0415
1781	    _sig = _observe(text)
1782	    _binding, _binding_ev = _bind(requested_doi=work.doi or '', requested_title=work.title or '',
1783	                                  requested_authors=list(work.authors or []), prof=_sig)
1784	    prof['semantic_binding'] = _binding
1785	    prof['semantic_binding_basis'] = _binding_ev
1786	    prof['semantic_binding_derived_by'] = 'ingest_bytes:derive_binding_core'
1787	
1788	    if prof['artifact_kind'] in ('wrong_work', 'landing_page', 'extraction_failure') \
1789	            or _binding == 'DIFFERENT_WORK':
1790	        # NOT A RENDERING OF ANY VERSION OF THIS WORK. It gets a QUARANTINE expression, not a version
1791	        # node: the ORA landing page for Frey & Osborne contains the words "published version
1792	        # (refereed)", and if we let that furniture name the expression we would have filed a WEB PAGE
1793	        # as an accepted manuscript of a journal article. The bytes are RETAINED in full (nothing is
1794	        # ever deleted) and attributable to nobody.
1795	        if _binding == 'DIFFERENT_WORK':
1796	            # A STRANGER'S PAPER, proven positively (foreign front-matter DOI, or a disjoint byline).
1797	            # It may render perfectly — as SOMEONE ELSE'S work. It creates no edge to the claimed
1798	            # expression (below) and names nobody.
1799	            eid = f'{wid}:quarantine:different_work'
1800	            att = (f'THESE BYTES ARE A DIFFERENT WORK than the one requested — '
1801	                   f'{_binding_ev.get("reason", "positive evidence of a stranger")} — no attribution '
1802	                   f'to this Work is possible')
1803	            _qbasis = str(_binding_ev.get('reason', 'different_work'))
1804	        else:
1805	            eid = f'{wid}:quarantine:{prof["artifact_kind"]}'
1806	            att = (f'THESE BYTES ARE NOT A USABLE RENDERING OF THIS WORK '
1807	                   f'({prof["artifact_kind"]}) — no attribution is possible')
1808	            _qbasis = prof['artifact_kind_basis']

1810	            id=eid, work_id=wid, kind='unknown', kind_basis=_qbasis,
1811	            attribution=att)

1813	        eid = f'{wid}:unresolved_version'

1815	            id=eid, work_id=wid, kind='unknown', kind_basis=ebasis,
1816	            attribution=_attribution_for('unknown', work))
1817	    else:
1818	        eid = f'{wid}:{ekind}'

1820	            id=eid, work_id=wid, kind=ekind, kind_basis=ebasis,
1821	            attribution=_attribution_for(ekind, work)))

1823	    h = hashlib.sha256(text.encode('utf-8', 'ignore')).hexdigest()
1824	    mid = f'manif:{h[:12]}'
1825	    g.manifestations[mid] = Manifestation(
1826	        id=mid, expression_id=eid, work_id=wid, text=text, content_hash=h,
1827	        n_words=prof['n_words'], locator=locator, locator_status=locator_status,
1828	        fetched_by=fetched_by, text_field=text_field, profile=prof)
1829	
1830	    # ---- EDGES. Only what the bytes support. ----------------------------------------------------
1831	    # An edge needs somewhere to point. If the row claimed no published expression, there is no target
1832	    # -- and inventing one to have something to point AT is how the journal_version node came to exist
1833	    # for every row in the first place.
1834	    if claimed_id and eid != claimed_id and not eid.startswith(f'{wid}:quarantine'):

1836	        def _edge(src, dst, typ, status, basis):

1838	                g.add_edge(src, dst, typ, status, basis)
1839	                existing.add((src, dst, typ))
1840	        if ekind in ('working_paper', 'preprint'):
1841	            # The strongest thing that is TRUE. Not exact_copy_of. Not even asserted: we have not
1842	            # compared the two texts, because we do not hold the other one.
1843	            _edge(eid, claimed_id, 'predecessor_of', 'PROPOSED',
1844	                  f'same work per bibliography metadata; version equivalence NOT tested '
1845	                  f'(we do not hold the {claimed_kind} bytes). {ebasis}')
1846	            _edge(eid, claimed_id, 'reports_same_study', 'PROPOSED',
1847	                  'same title/authors/DOI row; peer review may still have changed the numbers')
1848	        elif ekind == 'accepted_manuscript':
1849	            # PROPOSED, not ASSERTED: the artifact SAYS it is the accepted manuscript. That is a claim
1850	            # a component makes about itself, which is the one thing we never trust. ASSERTING this
1851	            # edge requires authentication against the journal version's bytes.
1852	            _edge(eid, claimed_id, 'accepted_manuscript_of', 'PROPOSED',
1853	                  f'the artifact self-describes ({ebasis}); NOT authenticated against the '
1854	                  f'{claimed_kind}, whose bytes we do not hold')
1855	        elif ekind == 'unknown':
1856	            _edge(eid, claimed_id, 'reports_same_study', 'PROPOSED',
1857	                  'the bytes carry no version furniture; they may or may not be the '

1862	def migrate(corpus: list[dict]) -> Graph:
1863	    g = Graph()
1864	    for row in corpus:

1868	            venue=row.get('venue') or '', source_type=str(row.get('type') or ''))
1869	
1870	        # ---- the bytes we hold, if any ----------------------------------------------------------

1872	            text = (row.get(fieldname) or '').strip()
1873	            if not text:

1875	            # The row's abstract came from the BIBLIOGRAPHY, independently of whatever body we

1877	            # never used to probe itself.

1881	            # records WHICH FETCHER RAN, not what the artifact is: it is false on 5 of the 6 rows
1882	            # that carry it. The bytes decide; the label is recorded and contradicted in the open.
1883	            claimed_src = row.get('fulltext_source') or 'publisher_or_oa'
1884	
1885	            # THE LOCATOR. wp_fetch.py NEVER RECORDED THE URL IT FETCHED FROM, so for the rows it
1886	            # touched the only URL on the row is `oa_url` -- left by an EARLIER fetcher and pointing
1887	            # at the PUBLISHER (aeaweb.org!). A locator that names the journal while the bytes are a
1888	            # working paper is worse than no locator, so it is not silently adopted.

    # The version reducer USED to read one set of stamps here (_PUBLISHED_STAMP/_ACCEPTED_STAMP/
    # _PREPRINT_STAMP) while provenance.derive_expression_kind read a DIFFERENT set (_JOURNAL_MARK/

    # ONE reducer over ONE vocabulary and can never diverge again.

input.old_string: def observe_text(text: str, header_chars: int = 1500) -> dict:
input.new_string:
def version_furniture(text: str) -> dict:
    """THE ONE VERSION-FURNITURE OBSERVER (Sol P2). Booleans only — it CONCLUDES NOTHING.

    Read with the SAME provenance marks, the SAME windows, and the SAME cover-sheet segmentation that
    `provenance.derive_expression_kind` has always used, so the two lanes cannot diverge:

      * am / wp / preprint stamps are read from the whole head (a library describing the file it
        deposited is testimony about these bytes — the cover sheet MAY CONVICT);
      * journal furniture is read ONLY from the article's own body, with the cover sheet segmented off
        (a cover sheet's DOI / citation block is the library CITING the article of record, which is the
        one thing the article of record itself never has to do — the cover sheet MAY NOT ACQUIT).

    `version_evidence()` maps these booleans to a VersionDecision; this function decides nothing.
    """
    head = text[:12000]
    _cover, document = _segment_cover_sheet(head)
    return {
        'am':       bool(_ACCEPTED_STAMP.search(head)),
        'wp':       bool(_WP_MARK.search(head)),
        'preprint': bool(_PREPRINT_MARK.search(head)),
        'journal':  bool(_JOURNAL_MARK.search(document[:12000])),
        'has_cover_sheet': bool(_cover),
    }

1132	#:
1133	#: THE ORDER OF THIS TUPLE IS THE RULE, AND IT IS A STATEMENT — not an accident of where an `if` landed
1134	#: in a ladder. The INADMISSIBLE kinds are asked FIRST, on purpose, because the commonest shape in an
1135	#: institutional repository carries BOTH kinds of stamp at once:
1136	#:
1137	#:     "This is an author produced version of a paper published in <journal>.     <- ACCEPTED
1138	#:      Citation for the published version: ..."                                  <- PUBLISHED
1139	#:
1140	#: The old reducer asked `published_stamp_in_header` first and answered VERSION_PUBLISHED. Reading the
1141	#: SECOND line and ignoring the FIRST is how an accepted manuscript became journal evidence. Whichever
1142	#: way a future edit reorders the code, the precedence lives HERE, in data, and `derive_eligibility`
1143	#: re-checks the result against a veto that does not consult this order at all.
1144	#:
1145	#: (`*_in_cover` is the LIBRARY's voice, `*_in_header` the DOCUMENT's own. For the two inadmissible
1146	#: kinds EITHER voice convicts — a repository saying "this is the author's manuscript" is describing

#: ══ WHICH EXPRESSION DO THESE BYTES TESTIFY TO BEING? ════════════════════════════════════════════
#:
#: THE ORDER OF THIS TUPLE IS THE RULE, AND IT IS A STATEMENT — not an accident of where an `if` landed
#: in a ladder. The INADMISSIBLE kinds are asked FIRST, on purpose, because the commonest shape in an
#: institutional repository carries BOTH kinds of stamp at once:
#:
#:     "This is an author produced version of a paper published in <journal>.     <- ACCEPTED
#:      Citation for the published version: ..."                                  <- PUBLISHED
#:
#: The old reducer asked `published_stamp_in_header` first and answered VERSION_PUBLISHED. Reading the
#: SECOND line and ignoring the FIRST is how an accepted manuscript became journal evidence. Whichever
#: way a future edit reorders the code, the precedence lives HERE, in data, and `derive_eligibility`
#: re-checks the result against a veto that does not consult this order at all.
#:
#: (`*_in_cover` is the LIBRARY's voice, `*_in_header` the DOCUMENT's own. For the two inadmissible
#: kinds EITHER voice convicts — a repository saying "this is the author's manuscript" is describing
#: the file it filed. For PUBLISHED, either voice may acquit, but only because nothing above it fired.)
VERSION_EVIDENCE: tuple[tuple[str, tuple[str, ...], str], ...] = (
    (VERSION_ACCEPTED,  ('accepted_stamp_in_cover', 'accepted_stamp_in_header'),
     'the bytes (or the repository that filed them) declare this an ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT — acceptance '
     'precedes copy-editing, proofs and the editor\'s last round, and THE NUMBERS MOVE ACROSS THEM'),
    (VERSION_PREPRINT,  ('preprint_stamp_in_cover', 'preprint_stamp_in_header'),
     'the header carries a preprint/working-paper stamp — peer review CHANGES NUMBERS; this is a '
     'DIFFERENT SOURCE until version equivalence is proven'),
    (VERSION_PUBLISHED, ('published_stamp_in_cover', 'published_stamp_in_header'),
     'a published-version/refereed stamp, and NO accepted-manuscript or preprint stamp contradicting it'),
)


def version_evidence(prof: dict) -> tuple[str, str]:
    """-> (binding, why). The ONE place a stamp becomes a version. Consulted by nothing else."""
    for binding, fields, why in VERSION_EVIDENCE:
        if any(prof.get(f) for f in fields):
            return binding, why
    return SAME_WORK, 'no version furniture contradicts the identity we confirmed'

#: ══ WHICH EXPRESSION DO THESE BYTES TESTIFY TO BEING, AND WHAT SEMANTIC VERSION DOES THAT MAKE THEM? ═
#:
#: THE ONE VERSION REDUCER (Sol P2). It returns BOTH the semantic binding and the own-expression kind
#: from ONE pass over ONE set of observations, so `ingest_bytes` (which needs the expression kind) and
#: `derive_binding_core` (which needs the semantic binding) can never again be TWO derivations that
#: disagree. There is exactly one mapping from expression kind to semantic binding, below, and the
#: resolver's compatibility table is literally its inverse — so the pair the reducer emits is, by
#: construction, always a consistent pair, and the ONLY way an inconsistent pair can reach the resolver
#: is tampering or in-memory corruption, which is what DERIVATION_CONFLICT quarantines.
#:
#: THE ORDER OF THE RULES IS THE PRECEDENCE, AND IT IS A STATEMENT — not an accident of where an `if`
#: landed. Accepted / working-paper / preprint furniture VETOES published furniture, because the
#: commonest shape in an institutional repository carries BOTH at once:
#:
#:     "This is an author produced version of a paper published in <journal>.     <- ACCEPTED
#:      Citation for the published version: ..."                                  <- PUBLISHED
#:
#: Reading the SECOND line and ignoring the FIRST is how an accepted manuscript became journal evidence.
#: The precedence lives HERE, in data (accepted, then working paper, then preprint, then published, then
#: no evidence), and `derive_eligibility` / the resolver re-check the result against tables that do not
#: consult this order at all.

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class VersionDecision:
    """The ONE reducer's output: BOTH the semantic binding AND the own-expression kind, plus the
    evidence key that fired and a human basis. `ingest_bytes` assigns its expression kind from this;
    `derive_binding_core` uses its semantic binding when identity is proven; the two therefore agree
    by construction, never by coincidence."""
    semantic_binding: str
    expression_kind: str
    evidence_key: str
    basis: str


#: THE SINGLE MAPPING expression-kind -> semantic-binding. The resolver's COMPATIBLE_VERSION_PAIRS is
#: built as its inverse (below), so the reducer and the gate cannot drift: adding a version kind here
#: without deciding its binding is impossible, because both come from this one dict.
_EXPRESSION_TO_BINDING: dict[str, str] = {
    'journal_version':     VERSION_PUBLISHED,
    'proceedings_version': VERSION_PUBLISHED,
    'accepted_manuscript': VERSION_ACCEPTED,
    'working_paper':       VERSION_PREPRINT,
    'preprint':            VERSION_PREPRINT,
    'official_text':       SAME_WORK,
    'registry_record':     SAME_WORK,
    'unknown':             SAME_WORK,
}

#: THE ONE DECLARATIVE COMPATIBILITY TABLE (Sol P2). The resolver validates the pair
#: (semantic_binding, own_expression_kind) against THIS and nothing else. It is the inverse of the
#: reducer's own mapping, so a pair the reducer can emit is always in it, and a pair it cannot emit
#: (e.g. VERSION_OF_PREPRINT + journal_version) is a DERIVATION_CONFLICT — the medRxiv/working-paper-
#: as-journal P0, quarantined structurally.
COMPATIBLE_VERSION_PAIRS: frozenset[tuple[str, str]] = frozenset(
    (binding, kind) for kind, binding in _EXPRESSION_TO_BINDING.items())

#: Typed ordered rules — (evidence-furniture key, own expression kind, basis). The order IS the
#: precedence. Working-paper and preprint furniture are SPLIT structurally (two rules, two expression
#: kinds), though both map to VERSION_OF_PREPRINT. No DOI, title, author, journal or subject appears
#: here — only the structural furniture booleans version_furniture() observed.
VERSION_EVIDENCE: tuple[tuple[str, str, str], ...] = (
    ('am',       'accepted_manuscript',
     'the bytes (or the repository that filed them) declare this an ACCEPTED MANUSCRIPT — acceptance '
     'precedes copy-editing, proofs and the editor\'s last round, and THE NUMBERS MOVE ACROSS THEM'),
    ('wp',       'working_paper',
     'the header carries a working-paper stamp — peer review CHANGES NUMBERS; this is a DIFFERENT '
     'SOURCE until version equivalence is proven'),
    ('preprint', 'preprint',
     'the header carries a preprint stamp — peer review CHANGES NUMBERS; this is a DIFFERENT SOURCE '
     'until version equivalence is proven'),
    ('journal',  'journal_version',
     'typeset journal furniture in the article\'s OWN front matter (not a cover sheet), and NO '
     'accepted-manuscript or working-paper or preprint stamp contradicting it'),
)


def version_evidence(prof: dict, work_kind: str = 'study') -> VersionDecision:
    """THE ONE PLACE version furniture becomes a (semantic binding, expression kind) pair.

    For a non-scholarly TYPED work (case / statute / trial) the version taxonomy is not the scholarly
    one — there is no preprint of a statute — so the expression is derived from `Work.kind` via the
    registry, never from scholarly furniture the opinion happens to quote. For a scholarly work the
    typed ordered rules (VERSION_EVIDENCE) decide, in precedence order.
    """
    fam = _WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind or 'study')
    if fam is not None:
        ek = KIND_PROFILE[fam]['expression']    # official_text / registry_record
        return VersionDecision(
            _EXPRESSION_TO_BINDING[ek], ek, f'work_kind:{work_kind}',
            f'work kind `{work_kind}` — the version taxonomy of a {fam.replace("_", " ")} is not the '
            f'scholarly one; these bytes are its {ek.replace("_", " ")}')
    furn = prof.get('version_furniture') or {}
    for key, ek, why in VERSION_EVIDENCE:
        if furn.get(key):
            return VersionDecision(_EXPRESSION_TO_BINDING[ek], ek, key, why)
    return VersionDecision(SAME_WORK, 'unknown', 'none',
                           'no self-identifying version furniture — the version is UNRESOLVED, though '
                           'the identity is not')

def derive_expression_kind(text: str, work_kind: str = 'study') -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Which VERSION do these bytes say they are? Derived from self-identifying furniture.

    Returns ('unknown', ...) freely. `unknown` is the honest answer for a PDF body with its title
    page mangled, and an honest `unknown` costs us evidence -- which is the correct price.

    THE SCHOLARLY VERSION TAXONOMY APPLIES ONLY TO SCHOLARLY WORKS. There is no preprint of a statute
    and no accepted manuscript of a judicial opinion; the authoritative text IS the artifact, and the
    registry says so. Running the NBER/arXiv/"accepted manuscript" regexes over a court opinion does
    not find nothing — it finds whatever the opinion happens to QUOTE, which is how a whole-text scan
    for "NBER Working Paper" once read the bibliography and called the JEP article a working paper.
    """
    fam = WORK_KIND_ARTIFACT.get(work_kind or 'study')
    if fam is not None:
        ex = KIND_PROFILE[fam]['expression']
        return ex, (f'work kind `{work_kind}` — the version taxonomy of a {fam.replace("_", " ")} is '
                    f'not the scholarly one; these bytes are its {ex.replace("_", " ")}')
    head = text[:12000]
    # THE COVER SHEET MAY CONVICT BUT MAY NEVER ACQUIT (see `_COVER_SHEET`). The three INADMISSIBLE
    # marks are read from the whole head — a library describing the file it deposited is testimony
    # about these bytes. The one ADMITTING mark is read ONLY from the article's own front matter,
    # because a cover sheet's DOI and citation block are the library CITING the article of record,
    # which is the one thing the article of record itself never has to do.
    cover, document = segment_cover_sheet(head)
    wp, pre, am = _WP_MARK.search(head), _PREPRINT_MARK.search(head), _AM_MARK.search(head)
    jr = _JOURNAL_MARK.search(document[:12000])

    # Order still matters (an accepted manuscript carries the journal's citation block too), but it is
    # no longer LOAD-BEARING: the only branch that can return `journal_version` cannot see the cover
    # sheet at all, so no reordering of these four lines can promote a manuscript to the article of
    # record. The ordering is a courtesy to the ERROR MESSAGE, not the thing keeping the door shut.
    if am:
        return 'accepted_manuscript', f'bytes say: {am.group(0)[:48]!r}'
    if wp:
        return 'working_paper', f'bytes say: {wp.group(0)[:48]!r}'
    if pre:
        return 'preprint', f'bytes say: {pre.group(0)[:48]!r}'
    if jr:
        return 'journal_version', (f'typeset journal furniture in the ARTICLE\'S OWN front matter '
                                   f'(not on a cover sheet): {jr.group(0)[:48]!r}')
    if cover:
        return 'unknown', ('a repository cover sheet, and NO version furniture in the document under '
                           'it — the library\'s citation of the article is not the article')
    return 'unknown', 'no self-identifying version furniture in the first 12,000 chars'

def derive_expression_kind(text: str, work_kind: str = 'study') -> tuple[str, str]:
    """Which VERSION do these bytes say they are? A COMPATIBILITY WRAPPER over the ONE reducer (Sol P2).

    This USED to be a second, independent derivation — it read `_WP_MARK`/`_PREPRINT_MARK`/`_AM_MARK`/
    `_JOURNAL_MARK` here, while `event_ledger.version_evidence` read a DIFFERENT set of stamps there,
    and the two could disagree about the very same bytes. They are now ONE reducer:
    `event_ledger.version_furniture()` reads exactly these marks (imported from this module) and
    `event_ledger.version_evidence()` maps them to a VersionDecision. This wrapper returns that
    decision's expression kind and basis, so every caller — `profile()`, the census, the P0-hop
    probes — sees the same answer the semantic-binding lane sees.

    THE SCHOLARLY VERSION TAXONOMY APPLIES ONLY TO SCHOLARLY WORKS. There is no preprint of a statute
    and no accepted manuscript of a judicial opinion; the authoritative text IS the artifact, and the
    registry (via `Work.kind`) says so — the reducer derives `official_text`/`registry_record` from the
    typed work, never from scholarly furniture the opinion happens to quote.
    """
    from event_ledger import version_furniture, version_evidence  # noqa: PLC0415
    dec = version_evidence({'version_furniture': version_furniture(text)}, work_kind)
    return dec.expression_kind, dec.basis
